options.en: Complement is sister of the lexical item (V); specifier is sister of X′; adjunct is sister of XP

options.zh: 補語是 XP 的姊妹；指定語是 V 的姊妹；附加語是 X′ 的姊妹
options.en: Complement is sister of XP; specifier is sister of V; adjunct is sister of X′

options.zh: 三者都是中心語 V⁰ 的姊妹
options.en: All three are sisters of the head V⁰

options.zh: 補語是 X′ 的姊妹；指定語是 XP 的姊妹；附加語是 V 的姊妹
options.en: Complement is sister of X′; specifier is sister of XP; adjunct is sister of V

explanationZh: Adger 採取的對應關係是：補語是詞項（中心語）的姊妹、指定語是 X′（bar-level）節點的姊妹、附加語是 XP 節點的姊妹，三者形成平行的結構關係。
explanationEn: Adger's parallelism is: complements are sisters of lexical items (heads); specifiers are sisters of X′ nodes; adjuncts are sisters of XP nodes.

questionZh: 在傳統 X-bar 理論中，每個詞組都會投射經過所有層級（XP 直接支配 X′，X′ 直接支配 X⁰），不論是否有指定語或補語；而 Adger 的系統則只在有指定語時才出現 bar-level 投射。
questionEn: In traditional X-bar theory every phrase projects through all levels (XP immediately dominates X′, which dominates X⁰), whether or not there is a specifier or complement; whereas in Adger's system bar-level projections appear only when there is a specifier.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: X-bar 理論強制投射經過所有層級，使樹更一致但層次更多；Adger 的系統因詞組地位由特徵衍生，bar-level 投射只在有指定語時出現，僅有補語或無補語/指定語時補語或中心語自動成為詞組。
explanationEn: X-bar theory enforces projection through all levels, yielding uniform but more layered trees; in Adger's feature-derived system bar-level projections appear only when there is a specifier, and otherwise the complement (or head) is automatically phrasal.

questionZh: Adger 對 c-command（成分統御）的定義為何？
questionEn: What is Adger's definition of c-command?

options.zh: 節點 A 統御 B，當且僅當 A 的姊妹「是 B」或「包含 B」
options.en: A node A c-commands B if and only if A's sister either is B or contains B

options.zh: 節點 A 統御 B，當且僅當 A 在線性順序上先於 B
options.en: A c-commands B iff A linearly precedes B

options.zh: 節點 A 統御 B，當且僅當 A 支配（dominate）B
options.en: A c-commands B iff A dominates B

options.zh: 節點 A 統御 B，當且僅當 A 與 B 共享相同的 φ-特徵
options.en: A c-commands B iff A and B share the same φ-features

explanationZh: c-command 是 sisterhood 的延伸：A 統御 B 當且僅當 A 的姊妹「就是 B」或「包含 B」。定義中不涉及線性順序，故節點可統御前置或後置的節點。
explanationEn: c-command is an extended sisterhood relation: A c-commands B iff A's sister either is B or contains B. The definition makes no reference to precedence, so a node can c-command preceding as well as following nodes.

questionZh: 在樹 [T [Z [X][Y]] [S [W][R]]] 中（Z 與 S 互為姊妹；X、Y 為 Z 的女兒；W、R 為 S 的女兒），節點 Z 統御（c-command）哪些節點？
questionEn: In the tree [T [Z [X][Y]] [S [W][R]]] (Z and S are sisters; X, Y are daughters of Z; W, R are daughters of S), which nodes does Z c-command?

options.zh: S、W、R
options.en: S, W, and R

options.zh: 只有 S
options.en: Only S

options.zh: X、Y
options.en: X and Y

options.zh: T、S、W、R
options.en: T, S, W, and R

explanationZh: Z 的姊妹是 S，故 Z 統御 S；又因 S 包含 W 與 R，Z 也統御 W 與 R。Z 不統御 X、Y（它們是 Z 的女兒，被 Z 包含而非被其姊妹包含）。
explanationEn: Z's sister is S, so Z c-commands S; since S contains W and R, Z also c-commands W and R. Z does not c-command X or Y (they are contained in Z itself, not in Z's sister).

questionZh: 在一棵樹中，最頂端的 ______ 節點（root node，如 T）既不統御任何節點、也不被任何節點統御，因為它包含所有其他節點而沒有姊妹。
questionEn: In a tree, the topmost ______ node (e.g. T) neither c-commands anything nor is c-commanded by anything, because it contains all the other nodes and so has no sister.
answerTextZh: 根（root）
answerTextEn: root
explanationZh: c-command 的定義仰賴姊妹節點；根節點沒有姊妹，因此既不統御任何節點，也不被任何節點統御。
explanationEn: c-command is defined via the sister node; the root node has no sister, so it neither c-commands nor is c-commanded by anything.

questionZh: 修訂後的反身代名詞概括（Reflexive Generalization）為何，並如何解釋 *Myself shaved me 的不合語法？
questionEn: What is the revised Reflexive Generalization, and how does it rule out *Myself shaved me?

options.zh: 反身代名詞必須與一個統御它的詞語同指；在 *Myself shaved me 中 me 並未統御 myself，故違反
options.en: A reflexive must be coreferential with a c-commanding expression; in *Myself shaved me, me does not c-command myself, so it is violated

options.zh: 反身代名詞必須與一個前置詞語同指；me 在 myself 之後，故違反
options.en: A reflexive must be coreferential with a preceding expression; me follows myself, so it is violated

options.zh: 反身代名詞必須與主語同指；myself 已是主語故合法
options.en: A reflexive must be coreferential with the subject; myself is the subject so it is fine

options.zh: 反身代名詞必須與動詞共享 φ-特徵；二者不符故違反
options.en: A reflexive must share φ-features with the verb; they mismatch so it is violated

explanationZh: 修訂版概括要求反身代名詞與一個統御它的詞語同指。賓語被主語統御但反之不然；在 *Myself shaved me 中，myself 為主語、me 為賓語，me 並未統御 myself，故反身代名詞缺乏統御它的先行詞，導致不合語法。
explanationEn: The revised generalization requires a reflexive to be coreferential with a c-commanding expression. An object is c-commanded by the subject but not vice versa; in *Myself shaved me, myself is the subject and me the object, so me does not c-command myself and the reflexive lacks a c-commanding antecedent.

questionZh: 為什麼 Adger 用 *The man I saw shaved myself 這個例子來主張反身代名詞概括應訴諸 c-command 而非「前置」（precedence）？請說明。
questionEn: Why does Adger use *The man I saw shaved myself to argue that the Reflexive Generalization must appeal to c-command rather than precedence? Explain.
answerTextZh: 若概括只要求反身代名詞與「前置」詞語同指，則 I 先於 myself 應使句子合法，但它不合法。透過成分性測試可知 the man I saw 是一個成分，I 被包含在這個 NP 之內；I 的姊妹不含 myself，含 myself 的節點也不被 I 的任何姊妹包含，故 I 並未統御 myself（雖然線性上前置）。因此真正起作用的是 c-command 而非 precedence。
answerTextEn: If the generalization only required a reflexive to be coreferential with a preceding expression, then I precedes myself and the sentence should be grammatical, but it is not. Constituency tests show the man I saw is a constituent containing I; I's sister does not contain myself, nor is the node containing myself contained in any sister of I, so I does not c-command myself (even though it precedes it). Hence the relevant notion is c-command, not precedence.
explanationZh: I 雖前置於 myself，卻被埋在主語 NP the man I saw 之內，因而其上方有一個不含反身代名詞的節點，使得 I 無法統御 myself。前置版本錯誤地預測句子合法；c-command 版本正確排除它，故取 c-command。
explanationEn: Although I precedes myself, it is buried inside the subject NP the man I saw, so there is a node above I that does not contain the reflexive, and I fails to c-command myself. The precedence version wrongly predicts grammaticality; the c-command version correctly rules it out, so c-command is adopted.

questionZh: 修訂後的否定極性項（NPI）概括如何解釋 *Any boy saw no one 的不合語法，而 No one wanted any cake 卻合法？
questionEn: How does the revised NPI Generalization explain that *Any boy saw no one is ungrammatical while No one wanted any cake is grammatical?

options.zh: NPI 必須被一個否定成分統御；在 No one wanted any cake 中主語 no one 統御賓語 any，但在 *Any boy saw no one 中 any 在主語位置未被 no one 統御
options.en: An NPI must be c-commanded by a negative element; in No one wanted any cake the subject no one c-commands the object any, but in *Any boy saw no one the NPI any is in subject position and is not c-commanded by no one

options.zh: NPI 必須前置於否定成分；any boy 前置故合法
options.en: An NPI must precede the negative element; any boy precedes so it is fine

options.zh: NPI 只能出現在賓語位置，不能在主語位置
options.en: NPIs may only occur in object position, never subject position

options.zh: 句中只要含 no one 即為否定句，故兩句都應合法
options.en: Any sentence containing no one is negative, so both should be grammatical

explanationZh: 修訂版 NPI 概括要求 NPI 被否定成分統御。No one wanted any cake 中主語 no one 統御賓語 any，合法；*Any boy saw no one 中 any 在主語位置，不被賓語 no one 統御，故被排除。可見關鍵是 c-command 這個結構概念，而非單純的線性或語意。
explanationEn: The revised NPI Generalization requires an NPI to be c-commanded by a negative element. In No one wanted any cake the subject no one c-commands the object any (grammatical); in *Any boy saw no one the NPI any is the subject and is not c-commanded by the object no one (ruled out). The relevant notion is the structural one of c-command, not mere linear order or negativity.

questionZh: 一個「缺少介系詞」的雙及物結構（如 Benjamin gave Lee the cloak）被稱為 ______ 結構；其中的 Goal 通常稱為「間接賓語」。
questionEn: A ditransitive that lacks a preposition (e.g. Benjamin gave Lee the cloak) is called a ______ construction; its Goal is usually called the indirect object.
answerTextZh: 雙賓（double object）
answerTextEn: double object
explanationZh: 雙賓結構是缺少介系詞的雙及物結構（如 Benjamin gave Lee the cloak），其中的 Goal 通常稱為間接賓語；它常有一個含介系詞的對應句（gave the cloak to Lee）。
explanationEn: A double object construction is a ditransitive lacking a preposition (e.g. Benjamin gave Lee the cloak), in which the Goal is the indirect object; it usually has a corresponding prepositional version (gave the cloak to Lee).

questionZh: 在判定雙及物 VP 結構時，哪一項證據支持「賓語與 Goal 形成一個成分（即 ?P）」的二元分支分析？
questionEn: In determining the structure of a ditransitive VP, which evidence supports the binary analysis in which the object and the Goal form a constituent (the ?P)?

options.zh: 並列（co-ordination）：Sam gave [the cloak to Lee] and [the magic chalice to Matthew] 無需特殊停頓即可成立
options.en: Co-ordination: Sam gave [the cloak to Lee] and [the magic chalice to Matthew] is acceptable without special intonation

options.zh: VP-preposing 可以單獨前移 give the cloak
options.en: VP-preposing can move just give the cloak

options.zh: give the cloak 可被助動詞 do 替代
options.en: give the cloak can be replaced by the auxiliary do

options.zh: *Benjamin gave to Lee it 可接受
options.en: *Benjamin gave to Lee it is acceptable

explanationZh: 並列測試提供（雖然較弱的）證據：Sam gave [the cloak to Lee] and [the magic chalice to Matthew] 在沒有特殊停頓下成立，暗示賓語與 Goal 構成一個成分（?P）。動詞＋賓語的成分性則缺乏支持（VP-preposing、替代測試皆失敗）。
explanationEn: Co-ordination provides (admittedly weak) evidence: Sam gave [the cloak to Lee] and [the magic chalice to Matthew] is acceptable without special intonation, suggesting the object and Goal form a constituent (?P). The verb+object constituency, by contrast, is unsupported (VP-preposing and replacement tests fail).

questionZh: 在雙賓結構中，反身代名詞繫連的對比為 Emily showed Benjamin himself（合法）對比 *Emily showed himself Benjamin（不合法）。請說明這個不對稱如何幫助在三元分支與含 ?P 的二元結構之間做選擇。
questionEn: In double object constructions the reflexive binding contrast is Emily showed Benjamin himself (good) vs *Emily showed himself Benjamin (bad). Explain how this asymmetry helps choose between a ternary structure and the binary ?P structure.
answerTextZh: 在三元分支結構中，兩個賓語互為姊妹、彼此統御，故會錯誤預測反身代名詞在間接賓語位置（*showed himself Benjamin）也合法。將 Goal 右附加到 V 的二元分支也有同樣問題。唯有含 ?P 的二元結構（第一個賓語在 ?P 指定語、c-command 第二個賓語，反之不然）才能正確：Benjamin 統御 himself（showed Benjamin himself 合法），而 himself 不被 Benjamin 統御於 *showed himself Benjamin（排除）。故繫連不對稱支持含 ?P 的二元結構。
answerTextEn: In a ternary structure the two objects are sisters and c-command each other, wrongly predicting a reflexive in indirect-object position (*showed himself Benjamin) to be grammatical; the binary structure with the Goal right-adjoined to V has the same problem. Only the binary ?P structure works: the first object sits in the specifier of ?P and c-commands the second but not vice versa. So Benjamin c-commands himself (showed Benjamin himself, good), while in *showed himself Benjamin the antecedent does not c-command the reflexive (ruled out). The binding asymmetry thus supports the binary ?P structure.
explanationZh: 關鍵在於 c-command 的不對稱：?P 結構中第一個（線性在前的）賓語統御第二個，但反之不成立，正好對應反身代名詞只能由前者繫連後者的事實；三元與 V-adjunction 結構則使兩賓語互相統御，錯誤地允許 *showed himself Benjamin。
explanationEn: The key is the c-command asymmetry: in the ?P structure the first (linearly earlier) object c-commands the second but not vice versa, matching the fact that only the former can bind the latter; ternary and V-adjunction structures make the two objects mutually c-command, wrongly allowing *showed himself Benjamin.

questionZh: 對於帶介系詞 Goal 的雙及物句（如 *Emily showed himself to Benjamin），介系詞會阻擋其補語對動詞賓語的 c-command，因此這類例子無法用來在三元與二元分析之間做選擇。
questionEn: For ditransitives with a prepositional Goal (e.g. *Emily showed himself to Benjamin), the preposition blocks c-command from its complement to the verb's object, so such examples cannot be used to choose between the ternary and binary analyses.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 因為 Benjamin 被包在 PP 之內，無論採三元或二元分析，它都無法統御反身代名詞，故 *showed himself to Benjamin 的不合語法與兩種分析皆相容；只有「雙賓結構」（無介系詞）能據以區分結構。
explanationEn: Since Benjamin is contained inside the PP, it cannot c-command the reflexive under either a ternary or a binary analysis, so the ungrammaticality of *showed himself to Benjamin is consistent with both; only the double object (preposition-less) constructions can distinguish the structures.

questionZh: 說明 Adger 引進的「小 v（little v）」分析：小 v 編碼什麼語意？動詞（如 show）如何與 v 結合？這種分析叫什麼名稱？v 與 VP 的關係靠什麼來保證（而非 c-選擇）？
questionEn: Explain Adger's little v analysis: what semantics does little v encode, how does the verb (e.g. show) combine with v, what is this analysis called, and what guarantees the v–VP relation instead of c-selection?
answerTextZh: 小 v 是編碼「致使（causality）」的輕動詞。動詞（如 show）透過 Move 移位併入 v，形成 v+show 之類的複合，並在原位留下語跡（trace）；下層 VP 因此沒有外顯動詞。這種分層的動詞結構稱為 VP-shell 分析。v 與 VP 的關係不靠 c-選擇或 θ-角色，而由「投射層級」（Hierarchy of Projections, v > V）保證：只要有小 v，其下必有 VP 補語。
answerTextEn: little v is a light verb encoding causality. The verb (e.g. show) undergoes Move and adjoins to v, forming a complex like v+show and leaving a trace in its base position, so the lower VP has no overt verb. This layered verbal structure is the VP-shell analysis. The v–VP relation relies not on c-selection or a θ-role but on the Hierarchy of Projections (v > V): whenever there is a little v, it must take a VP complement.
explanationZh: 小 v 是編碼致使的「輕動詞」，動詞（如 show）經 Move 併入 v 形成 v+show 並留下語跡，這就是 VP-shell 分析。v 與 VP 的關係不靠選擇/θ-角色，而靠投射層級（Hierarchy of Projections: v > V），確保 v 之下必有 VP 補語。
explanationEn: little v is a causality-encoding light verb; the verb (e.g. show) moves and adjoins to v forming v+show and leaving a trace — the VP-shell analysis. The v–VP relation is not selection/θ-role but the Hierarchy of Projections (v > V), ensuring v always takes a VP complement.

questionZh: 為使 v 與 VP 的關係與選擇／θ-角色分離，Adger 假設有一個「投射層級」（______ of Projections），規定每當有小 v 時，它必定帶有一個 VP 補語，記作 v > V。
questionEn: To keep the v–VP relation distinct from selection/θ-roles, Adger posits a ______ of Projections stating that whenever there is a little v it always has a VP complement, written v > V.
answerTextZh: 層級（Hierarchy）
answerTextEn: Hierarchy
explanationZh: 因為 v 並未對 VP 指派 θ-角色，Adger 不用 c-選擇，而引進 Hierarchy of Projections（v > V）來保證 v 之上必有、其下必帶 VP，否則結構被排除。
explanationEn: Since v assigns no θ-role to VP, Adger avoids c-selection and introduces the Hierarchy of Projections (v > V) to guarantee that a little v always sits above a VP complement; otherwise the structure is ruled out.

questionZh: 採用「小 v＋動詞移位」的 VP-shell 分析有何理論上的好處與代價？請說明。
questionEn: What is the theoretical benefit and the cost of adopting the VP-shell analysis with little v plus verb movement? Explain.
answerTextZh: 好處：系統可以只維持單一版本、且只容許二元分支（binary branching）的 Merge，同時把雙及物結構與致使結構連結起來。代價：必須引進一個新的句法運作 Move。Adger 指出，從理論角度看，採「二元 Merge＋Move」是否真的優於「同時允許二元與三元 Merge」並不顯然，這是當下的理論成本。
answerTextEn: Benefit: the system can maintain a single, strictly binary-branching version of Merge while also linking ditransitive structures to causatives. Cost: it must introduce a new syntactic operation, Move. Adger notes it is not obvious that binary Merge plus Move is theoretically better than allowing both binary and ternary Merge, so this is a current theoretical cost.
explanationZh: VP-shell 分析讓我們維持只有二元分支的 Merge，並把雙及物與致使結構連結起來；但它引進了新運作 Move，這是當下的理論代價（相較於同時允許二元與三元 Merge）。
explanationEn: The VP-shell analysis lets us maintain a single binary-only Merge and links ditransitives to causatives, but it introduces the new operation Move, which is a theoretical cost (compared with allowing both binary and ternary Merge).

questionZh: UTAH（θ-角色指派一致性假設）的內容為何？
questionEn: What does the UTAH (Uniformity of Theta Assignment Hypothesis) state?

options.zh: 謂語與其論元之間相同的論旨關係，在合併（Merge）時以相同的結構關係來表徵
options.en: Identical thematic relationships between predicates and their arguments are represented by identical structural relationships when items are Merged

options.zh: 每個謂語都恰好指派三個 θ-角色
options.en: Every predicate assigns exactly three θ-roles

options.zh: Agent 一律出現在補語位置，Theme 一律出現在指定語位置
options.en: Agents always appear in complement position and Themes in specifier position

options.zh: θ-角色只能在 LF 層次指派
options.en: θ-roles can only be assigned at LF

explanationZh: UTAH 主張謂語與論元間相同的論旨關係，在 Merge 時對應到相同的結構配置。它是解決「連結問題」（Linking Problem）的方案，讓兒童能據 θ-角色預測句法結構。
explanationEn: UTAH holds that identical thematic relations between predicates and arguments are represented by identical structural relations at Merge. It is a solution to the Linking Problem, letting children predict syntactic structures from θ-roles.

questionZh: 依 Adger 為落實 UTAH 所提的論旨—句法連結，下列對應何者「正確」？
questionEn: According to Adger's thematic–syntactic linkings adopted to implement UTAH, which correspondence is CORRECT?

options.zh: vP 的女兒 NP → Agent；VP 的女兒 NP → Theme；V 的女兒 PP → Goal
options.en: NP daughter of vP → Agent; NP daughter of VP → Theme; PP daughter of V → Goal

options.zh: vP 的女兒 NP → Theme；VP 的女兒 NP → Agent；V 的女兒 PP → Goal
options.en: NP daughter of vP → Theme; NP daughter of VP → Agent; PP daughter of V → Goal

options.zh: VP 的女兒 NP → Agent；vP 的女兒 NP → Goal；V 的女兒 PP → Theme
options.en: NP daughter of VP → Agent; NP daughter of vP → Goal; PP daughter of V → Theme

options.zh: vP 的女兒 PP → Agent；VP 的女兒 PP → Theme；V 的女兒 NP → Goal
options.en: PP daughter of vP → Agent; PP daughter of VP → Theme; NP daughter of V → Goal

explanationZh: Adger 提出：vP 的女兒 NP 解讀為 Agent、VP 的女兒 NP 解讀為 Theme、V 的女兒 PP 解讀為 Goal。如此每個論旨角色都對應唯一的詞組結構配置，符合 UTAH。
explanationEn: Adger proposes: an NP daughter of vP is interpreted as Agent, an NP daughter of VP as Theme, and a PP daughter of V as Goal. Each θ-role corresponds to a unique phrase-structural configuration, satisfying UTAH.

questionZh: 依 UTAH，非作格動詞（unergative，如 laugh）與非賓格動詞（unaccusative，如 fall）的單一論元各自出現在什麼位置？請畫出（以標記括弧表示）兩者的概略 vP 結構並說明。
questionEn: Under UTAH, where does the single argument of an unergative verb (e.g. laugh) versus an unaccusative verb (e.g. fall) appear? Give the rough vP structures (as labelled bracketings) and explain.
answerTextZh: 非作格動詞的唯一論元是 Agent，須為 vP 的女兒（即 vP 指定語）：[vP [Agent] [v′ v laugh]]，V 無補語。非賓格動詞的唯一論元是 Theme，須為 VP 的女兒：[vP [v′ v [VP fall [Theme]]]]，其 v 為語意上非致使的，無指定語。直覺上前者意為「X 致使一個笑的事件」，後者意為「X 經歷一個非致使的下落事件」。
answerTextEn: An unergative's single argument is an Agent and must be a daughter of vP (the vP specifier): [vP [Agent] [v′ v laugh]], with V taking no complement. An unaccusative's single argument is a Theme and must be a daughter of VP: [vP [v′ v [VP fall [Theme]]]], where v is semantically non-causal and has no specifier. Intuitively the former means 'X causes an event of laughter' and the latter 'X undergoes an uncaused falling event'.
explanationZh: 因為 Agent＝vP 女兒、Theme＝VP 女兒（依 UTAH 連結），unergative 的論元放在 vP 指定語、unaccusative 的論元放在 VP 內。Adger 在兩者皆假設有 vP（以維持投射層級），但 unaccusative 的 v 非致使、無指定語。此結構差異預測二者有不同句法表現（如義大利語助動詞選擇）。
explanationEn: Because Agent = daughter of vP and Theme = daughter of VP (the UTAH linkings), the unergative argument sits in the vP specifier while the unaccusative argument sits inside VP. Adger assumes a vP in both (to maintain the Hierarchy of Projections), but the unaccusative v is non-causal with no specifier. This structural difference predicts the distinct syntactic behaviour (e.g. Italian auxiliary selection).

questionZh: 在 Adger 描述的系統整體架構中，與概念—意圖（C-I）系統及發音—感知（A-P）系統介接的兩個對象分別是什麼？
questionEn: In the overall architecture Adger describes, what are the two objects that interface with the Conceptual-Intentional (C-I) and Articulatory-Perceptual (A-P) systems respectively?

options.zh: 與 C-I 介接的是邏輯形式（LF）；與 A-P 介接的是 Spellout 點（其輸出為 PF）
options.en: LF (Logical Form) interfaces with C-I; the Point of Spellout (whose output is PF) interfaces with A-P

options.zh: 與 C-I 介接的是 PF；與 A-P 介接的是 LF
options.en: PF interfaces with C-I; LF interfaces with A-P

options.zh: 與 C-I 介接的是 numeration；與 A-P 介接的是 lexicon
options.en: The numeration interfaces with C-I; the lexicon with A-P

options.zh: 兩者都由單一的 Spellout 對象同時介接
options.en: Both are interfaced by a single Spellout object

explanationZh: 與 C-I（意義）系統介接的句法對象是邏輯形式 LF（通常為終端對象）；與 A-P（發音）系統相關的則是 Spellout 點，其經非句法運作後產生 PF（語音形式）。須區分 Spellout 點（仍是樹結構）與 PF（介面層）。
explanationEn: The syntactic object interfacing with the C-I (meaning) system is Logical Form, LF (usually the terminal object); for the A-P (sound) system there is the Point of Spellout, which after non-syntactic operations yields PF (Phonetic Form). One must distinguish the Spellout point (still a tree) from PF (the interface level).

questionZh: 在 Adger 的衍生式架構中，一個衍生若終止時所有不可詮釋特徵都已被核查，則稱為「收斂（converge）」；若終止時仍有未核查的不可詮釋特徵，則稱為「崩潰（crash）」。
questionEn: In Adger's derivational architecture, a derivation that terminates with all uninterpretable features checked is said to 'converge'; one that terminates with unchecked uninterpretable features is said to 'crash'.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 衍生由 numeration 經 Select 取出詞項，再以 Merge、Move、Adjoin 反覆運作；若終止且所有不可詮釋特徵都已核查則收斂（句子合語法），否則崩潰（不合語法）。
explanationEn: A derivation draws items from a numeration via Select, then applies Merge, Move, and Adjoin repeatedly; if it terminates with all uninterpretable features checked it converges (grammatical), otherwise it crashes (ungrammatical).

questionZh: 本章摘要列出若干現象與其解釋。請各舉一例：(a) 解釋反身代名詞分佈所用的概念；(b) 解釋雙及物句中並列與反身代名詞所用的結構；(c) 解釋非賓格與非作格動詞行為差異所用的假設。
questionEn: The chapter summary pairs phenomena with explanations. Give one for each: (a) the notion used to explain the distribution of reflexives; (b) the structure used to explain co-ordination and reflexives in ditransitives; (c) the hypothesis used to explain the differing behaviour of unaccusatives and unergatives.
answerTextZh: (a) c-command（與修訂後的反身代名詞概括）；(b) VP-shell 結構（含小 v 取 VP 為補語）；(c) UTAH（θ-角色指派一致性假設）。
answerTextEn: (a) c-command (with the revised Reflexive Generalization); (b) the VP-shell structure (little v taking a VP complement); (c) the UTAH (Uniformity of Theta Assignment Hypothesis).
explanationZh: 本章摘要表將反身代名詞與 NPI 分佈歸因於 c-command；雙及物中的並列與反身代名詞行為歸因於 VP-shell 結構；語序變異歸因於中心語—補語/指定語的線性化；雙及物語序歸因於 V 移入小 v 與投射層級；非賓格／非作格差異歸因於 UTAH。
explanationEn: The summary table attributes the distribution of reflexives and NPIs to c-command; co-ordination and reflexives in ditransitives to VP-shell structures; word-order variation to linearization of head-complement/specifier structures; ditransitive word order to V-to-v movement and the Hierarchy of Projections; and unaccusative/unergative differences to UTAH.

questionZh: 在 Adger (2003) 的分析中,整個句子的中心語(head)是哪一個範疇?
questionEn: In Adger's (2003) analysis, which category is the head of the whole sentence?

options.zh: V(動詞)
options.en: V (verb)

options.zh: T(時制)
options.en: T (Tense)

options.zh: N(名詞)
options.en: N (noun)

options.zh: Sentence(句子)本身是自成範疇,沒有中心語
options.en: Sentence is its own category with no head

explanationZh: 本章論證句子其實是 T 的投射(TP),主語在 TP 的指示語位置,vP 是 T 的補語。T 承載整句的時制特徵。
explanationEn: The chapter argues that sentences are projections of T (TP), with the subject in Spec-TP and vP as the complement of T. T hosts the tense features of the whole sentence.

questionZh: 把句子分析成有中心語(由 T 領導)而非「Subject + VP」這種無中心語結構,理論上的好處是什麼?
questionEn: What is the theoretical advantage of treating sentences as headed (by T) rather than as a headless 'Subject + VP' structure?

options.zh: 可以避免使用 Merge 運算
options.en: It avoids using the Merge operation

options.zh: 不需要為「Sentence」這個範疇額外增加新的語法規則,句子如同其他範疇一樣由中心語投射
options.en: It avoids adding a new grammar rule for the category 'Sentence'; the sentence projects from a head like every other category

options.zh: 可以讓主語不必獲得 θ-角色
options.en: It allows the subject to receive no θ-role

options.zh: 可以取消詞庫(lexicon)
options.en: It eliminates the lexicon

explanationZh: 若句子無中心語,語法就得有特殊規則來引入「Sentence」範疇並規定其屬性。若由 T 領導,只需在詞庫中放入 T,句子屬性就如 PP 投射自 P、NP 投射自 N 一樣自然投射。
explanationEn: A headless sentence would require a special rule introducing the category 'Sentence'. If T heads it, we just place T in the lexicon and sentence properties project just as PP projects from P and NP from N.

questionZh: 偽分裂句(pseudocleft)與 VP 提前等測試顯示,情態詞(modal)只與動詞構成成分,而不與整個 vP 構成成分。
questionEn: Pseudoclefting and VP-fronting tests show that a modal forms a constituent only with the verb, not with the whole vP.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 錯誤。'What Gilgamesh may do is [seek Ishtar]' 與 '[seek Ishtar], Gilgamesh may' 顯示動詞及其賓語構成的成分可從情態詞分離,因此情態詞是與整個 vP(而非只與動詞)構成成分。
explanationEn: False. 'What Gilgamesh may do is [seek Ishtar]' and '[seek Ishtar], Gilgamesh may' show the verb+object constituent can be separated from the modal, so the modal is in constituency with the whole vP, not just the verb.

questionZh: 「時制呼應」(Sequence of Tense)現象,例如 'I thought she might be pregnant' 合語法但 '*I thought she may be pregnant' 不合語法,被用來支持什麼結論?
questionEn: The Sequence of Tense phenomenon (e.g. 'I thought she might be pregnant' is fine but '*I thought she may be pregnant' is not) is used to support what conclusion?

options.zh: 情態詞沒有語意內容
options.en: Modals have no semantic content

options.zh: 情態詞在句法上分為現在式與過去式兩類(如 might/could 等屬過去式),且此分類獨立於其語意詮釋
options.en: Modals split syntactically into present and past forms (e.g. might/could are past), independently of their interpretation

options.zh: 主句動詞不能帶時制
options.en: Matrix verbs cannot carry tense

options.zh: 情態詞必須與 do 共現
options.en: Modals must co-occur with do

explanationZh: 過去式主句動詞會強迫嵌入子句帶過去式。might/could/would 在時制呼應中表現得像過去式,支持傳統把情態詞分為現在/過去兩欄的分類,即使 'She might be pregnant' 在語意上未必指過去。
explanationEn: Past-tense matrix verbs force past on the embedded clause. might/could/would behave as past forms under Sequence of Tense, supporting the present/past classification of modals even though 'She might be pregnant' need not denote a past situation.

questionZh: 當句子中有情態詞承載時制特徵時,主要動詞仍須另外標記時制,例如 '*Gilgamesh might loved Ishtar' 是合語法的。
questionEn: When a modal bears the sentence's tense features, the main verb must still be marked for tense, so '*Gilgamesh might loved Ishtar' is grammatical.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 錯誤。當有情態詞承載時制時,主要動詞保持不帶時制屈折,'*Gilgamesh might loved Ishtar' 不合語法。
explanationEn: False. When a modal bears the tense features, the main verb remains uninflected for tense; '*Gilgamesh might loved Ishtar' is ungrammatical.

questionZh: 在 'Enkidu did free animals' 中,時制標記出現在助動詞 did 上而非主要動詞上;這個強調用助動詞稱為______(英文一詞)。
questionEn: In 'Enkidu did free animals' tense is marked on the auxiliary did rather than on the main verb; this auxiliary is the ______ auxiliary (one English word).
answerTextZh: 強調用(emphatic)助動詞 do
answerTextEn: the emphatic auxiliary do
explanationZh: 強調用 do 出現在 vP 之外的位置承載時制特徵,與直接在動詞上標時制(freed/frees)形成對比,顯示時制可標在 vP 外的位置。
explanationEn: Emphatic do appears outside vP and carries the tense features, contrasting with tense marked directly on the verb (freed/frees), showing tense can be marked outside vP.

questionZh: 非限定標記 to(如 'She tried to leave')被當作 T 中心語的證據之一是:
questionEn: One piece of evidence that the infinitival marker to (as in 'She tried to leave') is a T head is that:

options.zh: to 可以與情態詞自由並列,如 'to may leave'
options.en: to can freely co-occur with modals, as in 'to may leave'

options.zh: to 與情態詞互補分布(如 '*to may leave'),且其後動詞必須是不帶時制的形式
options.en: to is in complementary distribution with modals (e.g. '*to may leave'), and the following verb must be untensed

options.zh: to 必須帶過去式屈折
options.en: to must carry past-tense inflection

options.zh: to 只能出現在主句中
options.en: to can only appear in matrix clauses

explanationZh: to 與情態詞互補分布('*to may leave'、'*to can leave'),且 '*to left' 不合語法、強調用 do 也不可出現('*to do go'),顯示 to 佔據替代時制特徵的 T 位置。
explanationEn: to is in complementary distribution with modals ('*to may leave', '*to can leave'), the verb must be untensed ('*to left'), and emphatic do is impossible ('*to do go'), showing to occupies the T position replacing tense features.

questionZh: 用一兩句話說明 Adger 如何區分「功能範疇」(functional categories)與「詞彙範疇」(lexical categories),並各舉一例。
questionEn: In a sentence or two, explain how Adger distinguishes functional categories from lexical categories, with one example of each.
answerTextZh: 功能範疇會投射但不指派 θ-角色(如 T、little v 對其補語),提供句法骨架;詞彙範疇 V、N、A、P 承載 θ-相關語意並指派 θ-角色。功能範疇有語意,但與 θ-相關語意性質不同。
answerTextEn: Functional categories project but do not assign θ-roles (e.g. T, and little v to its complement), providing a syntactic skeleton; lexical categories V, N, A, P carry θ-related semantics and assign θ-roles. Functional categories have semantics, but of a different, non-θ kind.
explanationZh: T 是典型功能範疇:領導投射但不指派 θ-角色。little v 雖對其指示語有 θ-關係,但對補語是非 θ 的,故也類似功能範疇。投射層級為 T > v > V。
explanationEn: T is the prototypical functional category: it heads a projection but assigns no θ-role. little v assigns a θ-role to its specifier but relates non-thematically to its complement, so it is akin to functional categories. The Hierarchy of Projections is T > v > V.

questionZh: 模里西斯克里奧爾語(Mauritian Creole)的例子 'mo ti mahze'(I [past] eat,「我吃過了」)如何支持本章的核心主張?
questionEn: How does the Mauritian Creole example 'mo ti mahze' (I [past] eat, 'I ate') support the chapter's central claim?

options.zh: 顯示克里奧爾語沒有主語
options.en: It shows creoles have no subject

options.zh: 顯示時制不必標在動詞上,而是以位於主語之後、動詞之前的獨立質詞(particle)標記,符合「時制標在 vP 外」的觀點
options.en: It shows tense need not be on the verb but is marked by a separate particle after the subject and before the verb, fitting the view that tense is marked outside vP

options.zh: 顯示克里奧爾語的動詞會做時制屈折
options.en: It shows creole verbs inflect for tense

options.zh: 顯示英語的 do 來自克里奧爾語
options.en: It shows English do derives from creoles

explanationZh: 模里西斯克里奧爾語動詞本身不標時制,時制由置於主語後、動詞前的質詞(如 ti 表過去)標記,正是「時制標在 vP 之外、結構較高處」的跨語言證據。
explanationEn: Mauritian Creole verbs do not inflect for tense; tense is marked by a preverbal particle after the subject (e.g. ti = past), exactly the cross-linguistic evidence for tense marked higher up, outside vP.

questionZh: 在以英語為基礎的克里奧爾語 Sranan 中,過去式標記 ben 可被表進行貌或未來的貌中心語與動詞分開,如 'mi ben e waka'。其中過去式標記是______、進行貌標記是______。
questionEn: In Sranan (an English-based creole), the past marker ben can be separated from the verb by aspectual heads, as in 'mi ben e waka'. The past marker is ______ and the progressive marker is ______.
answerTextZh: 過去式 = ben;進行貌 = e
answerTextEn: past = ben; progressive = e
explanationZh: 'mi ben e waka' = I [past] [prog] walk「我那時正在走」。過去 ben 與進行 e(及未來 o)為獨立中心語,顯示 vP 之上、T 之前可再 Merge 進貌中心語,呼應英語進行/完成助動詞的層級。
explanationEn: 'mi ben e waka' = I [past] [prog] walk. ben (past), e (prog), and o (future) are separate heads, showing further heads can Merge above vP before T, paralleling English Perf/Prog auxiliaries.

questionZh: Adger 主張主要動詞本身帶有範疇特徵 T,因為它們也可以做時制屈折。
questionEn: Adger argues that main verbs themselves bear the category feature T, because they too inflect for tense.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 錯誤。動詞需要範疇特徵 V(且其句法涉及 little v),而且動詞與情態詞、強調用 do、to 並非互補分布(可同句共現),故動詞不帶 T 範疇特徵。
explanationEn: False. Verbs need the category feature V (and their syntax involves little v), and they are not in complementary distribution with modals/emphatic do/to (they co-occur), so verbs do not bear category feature T.

questionZh: 本章引入的 Agree 運算與第三章的選擇特徵核查(在姊妹關係下進行)有何關鍵差異?
questionEn: How does the Agree operation introduced in this chapter differ crucially from selectional feature checking (under sisterhood) from Chapter 3?

options.zh: Agree 不需要任何配對特徵
options.en: Agree requires no matching feature

options.zh: Agree 在 c-command(成分統御)關係下核查非範疇特徵,而非僅限姊妹關係
options.en: Agree checks non-categorial features under c-command, not just sisterhood

options.zh: Agree 只能核查範疇特徵(c-選擇特徵)
options.en: Agree can only check categorial (c-selectional) features

options.zh: Agree 會刪除可詮釋特徵
options.en: Agree deletes interpretable features

explanationZh: 選擇(範疇)特徵在姊妹關係下核查;Agree 把核查擴展到 c-command 之下用於非範疇特徵(如時制)。姊妹關係其實是 c-command 的一個子型,故選擇核查是更局域版的 Agree。
explanationEn: Selectional (categorial) features check under sisterhood; Agree extends checking to c-command for non-categorial features (e.g. tense). Sisterhood is a subtype of c-command, so selectional checking is a more local version of Agree.

questionZh: 在「以賦值核查」(checking by valuing)的取向下,little v 上的時制特徵起初是什麼狀態,又如何取得值?
questionEn: Under the 'checking by valuing' approach, what is the initial state of the tense feature on little v, and how does it get a value?

options.zh: 起初已賦好 [past] 值,Agree 只是刪除它
options.en: It starts already valued as [past]; Agree merely deletes it

options.zh: 起初是不可詮釋且未賦值的 [uInfl: ],由 T 上的可詮釋時制特徵透過 Agree 同時核查並賦值
options.en: It starts as an uninterpretable, unvalued [uInfl: ], and is both checked and valued by the interpretable tense feature on T via Agree

options.zh: 起初是可詮釋的時制特徵
options.en: It starts as an interpretable tense feature

options.zh: 它永遠保持未賦值且不被核查
options.en: It always stays unvalued and unchecked

explanationZh: little v 帶不可詮釋、未賦值的 [uInfl: ]。T 帶可詮釋且已賦值的時制特徵(如 [past]),透過 Agree 核查並把值傳給 v,使 v 成為 [uInfl: past]。如此就不會先生成再排除錯配結構。
explanationEn: little v bears an uninterpretable, unvalued [uInfl: ]. T bears an interpretable, valued tense feature (e.g. [past]), and via Agree checks and values v as [uInfl: past]. This never generates the ill-formed mismatch in the first place.

questionZh: Adger 主張英語的動詞一致(verbal agreement)只限於現在式。在 'Enkidu misses Gilgamesh' 中,T 不帶時制特徵,而只帶一個______特徵,由它為 v 上的 Infl 賦值。
questionEn: Adger argues English verbal agreement is restricted to the present tense. In 'Enkidu misses Gilgamesh', T bears no tense feature but only a ______ feature, which values Infl on v.
answerTextZh: 數(number)特徵(如 [singular])
answerTextEn: a number feature (e.g. [singular])
explanationZh: 現在式時 T 只帶數特徵([singular]),它與 v 的 [uInfl: ] 配對賦值為 [uInfl: singular],最終把 v 唸成 -(e)s(misses)。語意規則把缺時制特徵的 T 詮釋為現在式。
explanationEn: In the present, T bears only a number feature ([singular]); it values v's [uInfl: ] as [uInfl: singular], spelled out as -(e)s (misses). Semantically, T lacking a tense feature is interpreted as present.

questionZh: 關於完成貌 have 與進行貌 be 助動詞,本章的分析為何?
questionEn: Concerning the perfect auxiliary have and progressive auxiliary be, what is the chapter's analysis?

options.zh: 它們都是 T 中心語,因此一句中只能有一個
options.en: They are both T heads, so only one can appear per clause

options.zh: have 帶可詮釋範疇特徵 [Perf]、be 帶 [Prog],它們不同於 T 也彼此不同,可共現且排序固定為 Modal–Perf–Prog
options.en: have bears interpretable category feature [Perf], be bears [Prog]; they differ from T and from each other, can co-occur, and order strictly Modal–Perf–Prog

options.zh: 它們不投射任何詞組
options.en: They project no phrase at all

options.zh: 它們會指派 Agent θ-角色
options.en: They assign the Agent θ-role

explanationZh: 助動詞可與情態詞、to 共現,故非 T(一句僅一個 T)。have=[Perf]、be=[Prog],各投射 PerfP/ProgP,並把 v 的 [uInfl] 賦值,使 v 唸成過去分詞或 -ing。投射層級:T > (Perf) > (Prog) > v > V。
explanationEn: Auxiliaries co-occur with modals and to, so they are not T (only one T per clause). have=[Perf], be=[Prog], projecting PerfP/ProgP, and value [uInfl] on v so v is spelled as past participle or -ing. Hierarchy: T > (Perf) > (Prog) > v > V.

questionZh: 在像 'I might have been flying helicopters' 這種有多個助動詞的句子中,時制標記總是出現在第一個助動詞上,且每個助動詞各自決定其後動詞性成分的形態(完成 have 後接過去分詞、進行 be 後接 -ing)。
questionEn: In sentences with several auxiliaries like 'I might have been flying helicopters', tense marking is always on the first auxiliary, and each auxiliary determines the morphology of the next verbal element (perfect have → past participle, progressive be → -ing).

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 正確。時制永遠標在最高(第一個)助動詞上;完成 have 後接過去分詞、進行 be 後接現在分詞(-ing)、情態詞後接動詞原形,順序固定為 Modal–Perf–Prog。
explanationEn: True. Tense is always on the highest (first) auxiliary; perfect have is followed by a past participle, progressive be by a present participle (-ing), the modal by a bare stem, in the fixed order Modal–Perf–Prog.

questionZh: 根據本章資料,英語的句子否定詞(sentential negation)not 在助動詞序列中出現的位置規律是:
questionEn: According to the data, the placement of English sentential negation not in an auxiliary sequence follows which generalization?

options.zh: 否定詞總是出現在所有助動詞之前
options.en: Negation always precedes all auxiliaries

options.zh: 否定詞總是緊跟在第一個助動詞之後
options.en: Negation always follows the first auxiliary

options.zh: 否定詞總是出現在主要動詞之後
options.en: Negation always follows the main verb

options.zh: 否定詞可任意出現在任何助動詞前後
options.en: Negation can appear freely before or after any auxiliary

explanationZh: 如 'Gilgamesh might not have been reading...',句子否定總是緊跟第一個(最高)助動詞之後;'*might have not...' 只有成分否定的解讀。
explanationEn: As in 'Gilgamesh might not have been reading...', sentential negation always follows the first (highest) auxiliary; '*might have not...' only has a constituent-negation reading.

questionZh: 把否定詞納入後,本章最終版的投射層級(Hierarchy of Projections)為:T > (___) > (Perf) > (Prog) > v > V。請填入括號中的範疇。
questionEn: After incorporating negation, the final Hierarchy of Projections is: T > (___) > (Perf) > (Prog) > v > V. Fill in the bracketed category.
answerTextZh: Neg(否定)
answerTextEn: Neg (Negation)
explanationZh: 由於否定詞位於 T 右側、Perf 左側,故 Merge 在 Perf 之上、T 之下,層級為 T > (Neg) > (Perf) > (Prog) > v > V。括號表選擇性投射。
explanationEn: Since negation is to the right of T and left of Perf, it Merges above Perf and below T, giving T > (Neg) > (Perf) > (Prog) > v > V. Bracketed projections are optional.

questionZh: 若 Neg 在層級上高於 Perf,為何仍能生成 'Gilgamesh has not read the cuneiform tablets'(has 在 not 之前)?
questionEn: If Neg is higher than Perf in the hierarchy, how can we still generate 'Gilgamesh has not read the cuneiform tablets' (has before not)?

options.zh: 否定詞向下移到動詞
options.en: Negation lowers onto the verb

options.zh: 最高的助動詞(此處 has)經由中心語移位上移並附接到 T,因而跨過否定詞
options.en: The highest auxiliary (here has) undergoes head movement and adjoins to T, thereby crossing negation

options.zh: Perf 其實高於 Neg
options.en: Perf is actually higher than Neg

options.zh: 主語移到 not 之後
options.en: The subject moves after not

explanationZh: 英語中最高的助動詞會像 V 移到 v 一樣,做中心語移位(Aux-to-T),附接到 T,因而上移到 not 的左邊。即使沒有否定詞,為求結構一致也假設此移位發生。
explanationEn: In English the highest auxiliary undergoes head movement (Aux-to-T) and adjoins to T, just as V moves to v, thus raising to the left of not. For uniformity this movement is assumed even when no negation is present.

questionZh: 英語主要動詞與助動詞不同,'*Gilgamesh flew not the broomstick' 不合語法,顯示主要動詞不上移到 T,而是留在低位。
questionEn: Unlike auxiliaries, '*Gilgamesh flew not the broomstick' is ungrammatical, showing English main verbs do not raise to T but stay low.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 正確。英語主要動詞在否定詞之後('*flew not' 不合法),不像助動詞會上移到 T。要否定主要動詞需用助動詞 do(Gilgamesh didn't fly...)。
explanationEn: True. English main verbs follow negation ('*flew not' is bad) and, unlike auxiliaries, do not raise to T. Negating a main verb requires do (Gilgamesh didn't fly...).

questionZh: 本章用來解釋「為何帶時制的助動詞上移到 T、而帶時制的主要動詞留在原位」的特徵性質是什麼?
questionEn: What feature property does the chapter use to explain why a tensed auxiliary raises to T but a tensed main verb stays in situ?

options.zh: 可詮釋性(interpretability)
options.en: Interpretability

options.zh: 強度(strength):強特徵必須與其核查對象局域(姊妹),因而觸發移位
options.en: Strength: a strong feature must be local (sister) to the feature it checks, triggering movement

options.zh: 格(Case)
options.en: Case

options.zh: θ-角色數目
options.en: Number of θ-roles

explanationZh: 強度以星號標記(如 [uInfl*])。在英語中,助動詞上 [uInfl] 被賦值為時制時是強的,須局域於 T,觸發移位;little v 上的時制值是弱的,故不移位。
explanationEn: Strength is marked by an asterisk (e.g. [uInfl*]). In English, the valued tense on an auxiliary's [uInfl] is strong and must be local to T, triggering movement; the tense value on little v is weak, so no movement.

questionZh: 英法差異:英語中助動詞上的時制值為強、little v 上為弱;而法語中,當不可詮釋 Infl 特徵被賦值為時制時,在助動詞與 little v 上「都」是______。
questionEn: English vs French: in English, tense valued on an auxiliary is strong but on little v is weak; in French, when uninterpretable Infl features are valued as tense, they are always ______ on both auxiliaries and little v.
answerTextZh: 強(strong)
answerTextEn: strong
explanationZh: 法語中 v 上的時制值也是強的,故主要動詞與助動詞都上移到 T,出現在否定詞 pas 之前(Jean n'aime pas Marie)。Swedish 則兩者皆弱,故都留在 not 之後。
explanationEn: In French the tense value on v is also strong, so main verbs and auxiliaries both raise to T, appearing before pas (Jean n'aime pas Marie). Swedish has both weak, so both stay after negation.

questionZh: 法語對比 'Jean n'aime pas Marie'(動詞在 pas 之前)與英語的差異,最直接證明了:
questionEn: The French contrast 'Jean n'aime pas Marie' (verb before pas) versus English most directly demonstrates:

options.zh: 法語沒有否定詞
options.en: French has no negation

options.zh: 法語主要動詞會上移到否定詞之前的位置(V-to-T 移位),而英語主要動詞不會
options.en: French main verbs raise to a position before negation (V-to-T movement), whereas English main verbs do not

options.zh: 法語動詞不帶時制
options.en: French verbs bear no tense

options.zh: pas 是主語
options.en: pas is the subject

explanationZh: 在法語中主要動詞 aime 出現在真正否定詞 pas 之前,顯示法語動詞做 V-to-T 移位;ne/n' 通常被視為次要,真正否定標記是 pas。'*Jean (ne) pas aime Marie' 不合法。
explanationEn: In French the main verb aime precedes the real negator pas, showing French verbs undergo V-to-T movement; ne/n' is treated as secondary, pas being the true negation marker. '*Jean (ne) pas aime Marie' is ungrammatical.

questionZh: 瑞典語(Swedish)在此參數類型中佔什麼位置?Adger 指出該強度參數表中有一個未被驗證的可能組合,那是什麼?
questionEn: Where does Swedish fit in this parametric typology, and what unattested combination does Adger note the strength-parameter table predicts but he knows of no instance of?
answerTextZh: 瑞典語的不可詮釋時制特徵(助動詞與 v 上)都是弱的,故助動詞與主要動詞都留在否定詞之後(om hon inte har köpt boken)。表中未被驗證的組合是:v 上時制值為強(主要動詞上移)但助動詞上為弱(助動詞不上移)的語言——Adger 不知有此類語言。
answerTextEn: Swedish has weak uninterpretable tense on both auxiliaries and v, so both auxiliaries and main verbs stay to the right of negation (om hon inte har köpt boken). The unattested cell is a language where tense on v is strong (main verbs raise) but tense on auxiliaries is weak (auxiliaries do not raise) — Adger knows of no such language.
explanationZh: 三語類型:English(Aux 強、v 弱)、French(都強)、Swedish(都弱)。第四種邏輯可能(v 強、Aux 弱)未見證據;若真為缺口,理論應原則上排除,但目前理論卻預測它可能存在。
explanationEn: Three-way typology: English (Aux strong, v weak), French (both strong), Swedish (both weak). The fourth logical option (v strong, Aux weak) is unattested; if a genuine gap, the theory should rule it out in principle, but as it stands it predicts such a language could exist.

questionZh: 根據本章,觸發 do-support(do 支撐)的語境有哪兩類?
questionEn: According to the chapter, do-support is triggered in which two types of context?

options.zh: 疑問句與祈使句
options.en: Questions and imperatives

options.zh: T 被否定詞與動詞隔開,以及 T 完全不 c-command 動詞(VP 提前與 VP 省略)
options.en: T is separated from the verb by negation, and T does not c-command a verb at all (VP-fronting and VP-ellipsis)

options.zh: 有情態詞時,以及有完成貌時
options.en: When there is a modal, and when there is a perfect

options.zh: 主語移位與賓語移位
options.en: Subject movement and object movement

explanationZh: do-support 出現在:(1)否定詞介於 T 與動詞之間('Gilgamesh did not fly the dragon');(2)T 不 c-command 動詞,如 VP 提前與 VP 省略。關鍵在 not 本身介於 T 與 little v 之間;單純的副詞 never 介入則不觸發 do-support。
explanationEn: do-support appears when (1) negation intervenes between T and the verb ('Gilgamesh did not fly the dragon'), and (2) T fails to c-command the verb, as in VP-fronting and VP-ellipsis. Crucially not itself must intervene between T and little v; a mere adverb like never does not trigger it.

questionZh: 在 Chomsky 早期(Syntactic Structures)的分析裡,do-support 是一種「最後手段」(last-resort)策略,當把時制附接到主要動詞的規則因故無法運作時才啟動。
questionEn: In Chomsky's early (Syntactic Structures) analysis, do-support is a last-resort strategy that kicks in when the rule attaching tense to the main verb fails to apply.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 正確。Chomsky 提出一條專門規則:若其他規則未能運作,就插入 do 來「拯救」句子,生成合語法結構,屬最後手段。
explanationEn: True. Chomsky proposed a specialized rule that inserts do to 'rescue' the sentence when other rules fail to apply, generating a well-formed structure — a last-resort strategy.

questionZh: 「附綴否定」(affixal negation)與「附綴時制」(affixal tense)兩種分析共同面臨的一個過度生成(overgeneration)問題是什麼?
questionEn: What overgeneration problem do both the affixal-negation and affixal-tense analyses share?

options.zh: 它們錯誤排除了所有否定句
options.en: They wrongly rule out all negative sentences

options.zh: 它們錯誤地預測 '*Gilgamesh didn't ate the honey' 這類同時有 do-support 又有屈折動詞的句子合語法
options.en: They wrongly predict sentences like '*Gilgamesh didn't ate the honey' (do-support plus an inflected verb) to be grammatical

options.zh: 它們無法生成任何疑問句
options.en: They cannot generate any questions

options.zh: 它們需要動詞上移到 T
options.en: They require the verb to raise to T

explanationZh: 兩種分析中,T 都已把過去特徵賦值給 little v(產生 ate),之後 do-support 又因 T[neg]/T 附綴而啟動,於是錯誤地生成 '*didn't ate'。它們無法解釋為何不能同時有 do-support 與屈折動詞。
explanationEn: In both, T values past on little v (giving ate), and then do-support applies because T[neg]/T is affixal, wrongly generating '*didn't ate'. Neither explains why do-support and an inflected verb cannot co-occur.

questionZh: 把英語動詞屈折分析成「T 向下移位(lowering)附接到 v」的取向,被本章拒絕的主要理由之一是:
questionEn: One main reason the chapter rejects analysing English verb inflection as 'T lowering and attaching to v' is that:

options.zh: 向下移位需要新的詞庫項目
options.en: Lowering requires new lexical items

options.zh: 它無法解釋為何 VP 提前時被提前的 VP 不帶屈折('...and [eat the honey] he did',而非 '*[ate the honey]'),且理論無法施加外加的運算順序
options.en: It cannot explain why a fronted VP is uninflected ('...and [eat the honey] he did', not '*[ate the honey]'), and the theory cannot impose extrinsic ordering on movements

options.zh: 向下移位會刪除主語
options.en: Lowering deletes the subject

options.zh: 它需要兩個 T 中心語
options.en: It requires two T heads

explanationZh: 若 T 在句法中向下附接到 v,則動詞會被屈折,VP 提前後應錯誤生成 '*[ate the honey] he'。要避免就得規定 T 附接只在 VP 提前/省略之後運作,但本章理論只有詞項屬性、Merge、Move、Adjoin,無法施加外加(extrinsic)順序。此外多數句法學家也排除向下移位。
explanationEn: If T lowers onto v in the syntax, the verb is inflected and VP-fronting should wrongly yield '*[ate the honey] he'. Avoiding this requires ordering T-suffixation after VP-fronting/ellipsis, but the theory only has lexical properties, Merge, Move, Adjoin and cannot impose extrinsic ordering. Many syntacticians also rule out lowering entirely.

questionZh: 本章最終採用的 do-support 分析引入「鏈」(chain)概念,並提出一條拼讀規則,其英文縮寫為______;該規則規定:在鏈 (T[tense], v[uInfl: tense]) 中,只有當 v 是 T 的姊妹的中心語時,才把時制特徵唸在 v 上。
questionEn: The do-support analysis the chapter ultimately adopts introduces the notion of a chain and a spellout rule abbreviated ______; it states that in a chain (T[tense], v[uInfl: tense]), the tense features are pronounced on v only if v is the head of T's sister.
answerTextZh: PTR(Pronouncing Tense Rule,時制唸讀規則)
answerTextEn: PTR (Pronouncing Tense Rule)
explanationZh: 鏈由 Agree 形成,每個鏈節須 c-command 下一節。PTR:唯有 v 為 T 姊妹的中心語時,才把時制唸在 v 上。否定句中 not 是 T 姊妹的中心語、VP 提前/省略中鏈被打斷或 v 不被唸出,故 PTR 失效,改由最後手段 do-support 接手。
explanationEn: Chains are formed by Agree, and each link must c-command the next. PTR pronounces tense on v only if v heads T's sister. In negatives not heads T's sister; in VP-fronting/ellipsis the chain is broken or v is not pronounced, so PTR fails and the last-resort do-support applies.

questionZh: 說明在 PTR(時制鏈)分析下,為何 VP 提前(VP-fronting)與 VP 省略(VP-ellipsis)都會觸發 do-support,即使這兩種語境都不必是否定句。
questionEn: Explain why, under the PTR (tense-chain) analysis, both VP-fronting and VP-ellipsis trigger do-support, even though neither context need be negative.
answerTextZh: VP 提前時,vP 被移動並附接到 TP 之上,T 不再 c-command v,鏈被打斷,PTR 無法施用,故由 do-support 接手。VP 省略時,little v 根本沒被唸出,而 PTR 是唸讀規則,無法把時制唸在不被唸出的 v 上,故同樣由 do-support 承載時制。兩者皆與否定無關,正好補足「附綴否定」分析的漏洞。
answerTextEn: In VP-fronting, vP is moved and adjoined above TP, so T no longer c-commands v; the chain is broken, PTR cannot apply, and do-support takes over. In VP-ellipsis, little v is not pronounced at all, and since PTR is a pronunciation rule it cannot pronounce tense on an unpronounced v, so do-support carries tense. Neither involves negation, which is exactly the gap the affixal-negation analysis could not cover.
explanationZh: 這顯示 PTR 取向在經驗上勝過附綴否定/附綴時制:它統一解釋了否定句、VP 提前、VP 省略三類 do-support,代價是引入語言特定的鏈與唸讀規則(理論上不夠精簡)。
explanationEn: This shows PTR empirically outperforms the affixal analyses: it uniformly captures do-support in negatives, VP-fronting, and VP-ellipsis, at the theoretical cost of a language-specific chain and pronunciation rule.

questionZh: 本章開頭指出主語位置的「明顯矛盾」是什麼?
questionEn: What is the 'apparent contradiction' about subject position raised at the start of the chapter?

options.zh: 主語在表面上出現在限定助動詞的左邊,但作為施事卻在 little v 的指示語(在助動詞所在的 T 之下)被合併
options.en: The subject surfaces to the left of the finite auxiliary, yet as Agent it is Merged in the specifier of little v, which is below T where the auxiliary sits

options.zh: 主語永遠不能帶有格位特徵
options.en: Subjects can never bear case features

options.zh: 賓語總是出現在主語的左邊
options.en: Objects always appear to the left of subjects

options.zh: 限定助動詞無法被合併進句子
options.en: Finite auxiliaries cannot be Merged into the clause

explanationZh: 施事主語在 little vP 指示語合併,理應在 T 中助動詞之右;但實際語序中主語在助動詞之左。Adger 以「兩個主語位置經移位相連」解決此矛盾。
explanationEn: An Agent subject is Merged in Spec-vP and should follow the auxiliary in T, yet it actually precedes it. Adger resolves this via two subject positions related by movement.

questionZh: 依本章主張,及物動詞的主語在 little vP 的指示語(底層主語位置)合併,再移到 TP 的指示語(表面主語位置)。
questionEn: According to the chapter, the subject of a transitive verb Merges in Spec-vP (the base subject position) and then Moves to Spec-TP (the surface subject position).

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 這正是 Adger 解決語序矛盾的方法:主語在 Spec-vP 合併,再移位至 Spec-TP,因此可在限定助動詞之左被讀出。
explanationEn: This is exactly how Adger resolves the word-order puzzle: the subject Merges in Spec-vP and raises to Spec-TP, so it is pronounced to the left of the finite auxiliary.

questionZh: 「The dragons had all eaten the pigs」這類所謂的「漂浮量詞」(floating quantifier),在 Adger 的分析中是如何產生的?
questionEn: How are so-called 'floating quantifiers' such as in 'The dragons had all eaten the pigs' derived in Adger's analysis?

options.zh: 量詞並未真的漂浮,而是在其補語 NP 移位時被「滯留」(stranded)
options.en: The quantifier does not really float; it is 'stranded' when its complement NP undergoes movement

options.zh: 有一條轉換規則把量詞往右移到句中某處
options.en: A transformational rule moves the quantifier rightward to some position in the sentence

options.zh: 量詞與整個 NP 一起移到 Spec-TP
options.en: The quantifier moves to Spec-TP together with the whole NP

options.zh: 量詞由 little v 指派賓格而停留原位
options.en: The quantifier stays in place because little v assigns it accusative case

explanationZh: Adger 採用「滯留」分析:量詞 all 取 NP 補語(all [the dragons]),當較低的 NP「the dragons」移到 Spec-TP 時,量詞被留在 Spec-vP,因而看似漂浮。
explanationEn: Adger adopts the stranding analysis: the quantifier all takes an NP complement (all [the dragons]); when the lower NP 'the dragons' raises to Spec-TP, the quantifier is left behind in Spec-vP, giving the float illusion.

questionZh: 在滯留分析下,可被滯留的量詞(如 all、both)與不可漂浮的量詞(如 most、many)結構上的差異是什麼?
questionEn: Under the stranding analysis, what is the structural difference between strandable quantifiers (e.g. all, both) and non-floating ones (e.g. most, many)?

options.zh: 可滯留的量詞取 NP 補語;不可漂浮的量詞取 N 補語
options.en: Strandable quantifiers take an NP complement; non-floating ones take an N complement

options.zh: 可滯留的量詞是中心語,不可漂浮的量詞是附加語
options.en: Strandable quantifiers are heads, non-floating ones are adjuncts

options.zh: 可滯留的量詞帶格位特徵,不可漂浮的量詞不帶
options.en: Strandable quantifiers bear case features, non-floating ones do not

options.zh: 兩者沒有結構差異,差別只在語意
options.en: There is no structural difference, only a semantic one

explanationZh: Adger 假設量詞都是中心語;差別在補語:all/both 取 NP 補語(如 [all [the dragons]]),使較低 NP 可獨立移位;most/many 取 N 補語,沒有可單獨移走的 NP,故不能漂浮。
explanationEn: Adger assumes quantifiers are heads; the difference is the complement: all/both take an NP complement (e.g. [all [the dragons]]), allowing the lower NP to move on its own, whereas most/many take an N complement, leaving no separate NP to strand.

questionZh: 在「The dragons simply all died out」中,量詞 all 出現在 vP 附加副詞 simply 的(左/右)________邊,因為 all 被滯留在 Spec-vP,而 simply 附加在更高的 vP 之上。
questionEn: In 'The dragons simply all died out', the quantifier all appears to the (left/right) ________ of the vP-adjoined adverb simply, because all is stranded in Spec-vP while simply adjoins to a higher vP.
answerTextZh: 右(right)
answerTextEn: right
explanationZh: all 被滯留在 Spec-vP,而副詞 simply 附加在 vP 之上,因此 all 出現在副詞的下方(右邊),符合預測。
explanationEn: The all is stranded in Spec-vP while the adverb 'simply' is adjoined above vP, so all appears below (to the right of) the adverb, as predicted.

questionZh: 在語法理論中,可填入表面主語位置但不從謂語獲得 θ 角色的元素稱為________;英語有兩種:there 與 it。
questionEn: An element that may fill the surface subject position but does not receive a θ-role from the predicate is called a(n) ________; English has two: there and it.
answerTextZh: 填補詞 / 虛主語(expletive)
answerTextEn: expletive
explanationZh: expletive(填補詞)如 there 與 it,佔據 Spec-TP 但不承載 θ 角色;Adger 用 tag-question 測試證明 there 是真正的結構主語。
explanationEn: An expletive such as there or it occupies Spec-TP but bears no θ-role; Adger uses the tag-question test to show that there is a genuine structural subject.

questionZh: Adger 用什麼測試證明虛主語 there 是真正的結構主語?
questionEn: What test does Adger use to show that the expletive there is a true structural subject?

options.zh: 附加問句(tag-question)操作:tag 取句子主語並加上否定助動詞,而 there 可被 tag 挑出(如 isn't there?)
options.en: The tag-question operation: a tag takes the clause's subject with a negative auxiliary, and there is picked out by the tag (e.g. isn't there?)

options.zh: 被動化測試
options.en: The passivization test

options.zh: 量詞滯留測試
options.en: The quantifier-stranding test

options.zh: ne-附著(ne-cliticization)測試
options.en: The ne-cliticization test

explanationZh: 附加問句取句子主語並前綴否定助動詞置於句尾。「There's going to be a party, isn't there?」中 there 被 tag 挑出,顯示它是結構主語。
explanationEn: Tag-questions take the clause's subject and prefix it with a negative auxiliary at the end. In 'There's going to be a party, isn't there?' the there is picked out by the tag, showing it is the structural subject.

questionZh: 依 (41),英語中主格(nominative case)與哪個中心語相關聯,並透過什麼操作賦值給 NP?
questionEn: According to (41), in English nominative case is associated with which head, and is valued onto an NP via what operation?

options.zh: 限定 T 帶 [ucase:nom],透過 Agree 賦值 NP 的格位特徵
options.en: Finite T bears [ucase:nom] and values an NP's case feature under Agree

options.zh: little v 帶 [nom],透過 Merge 賦值
options.en: little v bears [nom] and values it under Merge

options.zh: V 帶 [nom],透過中心語移位賦值
options.en: V bears [nom] and values it under head movement

options.zh: Perf 帶 [nom],透過 c-command 賦值
options.en: Perf bears [nom] and values it under c-command

explanationZh: Adger 主張限定 T 帶不可解讀的 [ucase:nom](縮寫 [nom]),透過 Agree 與其 c-command 的 NP 配對,將該 NP 的格位賦值為主格。
explanationEn: Adger holds that finite T bears the uninterpretable [ucase:nom] (abbreviated [nom]) and, via Agree with an NP it c-commands, values that NP's case as nominative.

questionZh: 格位特徵在格位配對關係中很特殊:配對的雙方(T 上的 [nom] 與 NP 上的 [case])都是不可解讀的,這與一般「一可解讀、一不可解讀」的配對不同。
questionEn: Case features are special in the checking relation: both members of the pair (the [nom] on T and the [case] on the NP) are uninterpretable, unlike the usual 'one interpretable, one uninterpretable' pairing.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: Adger 指出:過去配對總是一可解讀(帶值)、一不可解讀;但格位特徵兩端都不可解讀。這點被許多語言學家認為不直觀,並在第 8 章引發技術問題。
explanationEn: Adger notes that, until now, pairs always had one interpretable (valued) and one uninterpretable member; but with case both members are uninterpretable. Many linguists find this unintuitive, and it raises technical issues in Chapter 8.

questionZh: Adger 為何主張驅動「Spec-TP 必須被填」的不是 T 上 [nom] 的強度,而是另一個特徵(EPP)?冰島語的證據是什麼?
questionEn: Why does Adger argue that what forces Spec-TP to be filled is NOT the strength of [nom] on T but a separate (EPP) feature? What is the Icelandic evidence?

options.zh: 冰島語虛主語句中 Spec-TP 由虛主語 það 填補,主格名詞仍留在原位;故填補 Spec-TP 與主格賦值必須分開
options.en: In Icelandic expletive clauses Spec-TP is filled by the expletive það while the nominative nominal stays in situ; so filling Spec-TP must be separate from nominative valuation

options.zh: 冰島語沒有主格
options.en: Icelandic has no nominative case

options.zh: 冰島語的 T 永遠不帶格位特徵
options.en: Icelandic T never bears a case feature

options.zh: 冰島語主語永遠不移位
options.en: Icelandic subjects never move

explanationZh: 若是 [nom] 的強度迫使移位,則主格名詞應移到 Spec-TP。但冰島語顯示虛主語 það 在 Spec-TP,主格名詞 (Nom) 留在低位,且 Spec-TP 仍必須被填(刪去虛主語則不合語法)。故需獨立的 EPP 特徵 [uN*]。冰島語的與格主語(dative subjects)更進一步證明主格賦值與 Spec-TP 填補需分開。
explanationEn: If strength of [nom] forced movement, the nominative should move to Spec-TP. But Icelandic shows the expletive það in Spec-TP with the Nom nominal staying low, yet Spec-TP must still be filled (dropping the expletive is ungrammatical). This motivates a separate EPP feature [uN*]; dative subjects in Icelandic reinforce that nominative valuation and Spec-TP filling are distinct.

questionZh: Adger 主張限定 T 帶一個純形式的強選擇性 N 特徵,記作 [uN*],用以迫使某個名詞性元素出現在 Spec-TP。由於歷史因素,此特徵被稱為________特徵。
questionEn: Adger argues that finite T bears a purely formal strong selectional N feature, written [uN*], forcing a nominal to appear in Spec-TP. Because of a historical accident, this feature is called the ________ feature.
answerTextZh: EPP(擴充投射原則,Extended Projection Principle)
answerTextEn: EPP (Extended Projection Principle)
explanationZh: [uN*] 類似詞彙範疇的選擇性特徵,但因 T 是功能性中心語、無 θ 角色可指派,故它是純形式特徵;基於歷史因素被稱為 EPP 特徵。
explanationEn: [uN*] is analogous to a c-selectional feature but, since T is functional and assigns no θ-role, it is purely formal; for historical reasons it is called the EPP feature.

questionZh: 在虛主語句中,EPP 特徵透過合併虛主語(如 there)而被滿足;此分析假設虛主語沒有格位需核查。
questionEn: In an expletive clause, the EPP feature is satisfied by Merging in the expletive (e.g. there); this analysis assumes the expletive has no case to check.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 在 T 已合併並與底層主語 Agree 後,可不提升主語,改合併虛主語以滿足 EPP。此分析假設虛主語無格位,因此底層主語仍可獨立核查主格。
explanationEn: After T has Merged and Agreed with the base subject, instead of raising the subject, the expletive is Merged to satisfy EPP. The expletive is assumed to have no case, so the base subject can still check nominative independently.

questionZh: 依本章分析,賓格(accusative case)由哪個中心語透過 Agree 賦值給賓語?
questionEn: According to this chapter, which head values accusative case onto the object under Agree?

options.zh: little v(帶 [acc] 特徵)
options.en: little v (bearing an [acc] feature)

options.zh: T(帶 [acc] 特徵)
options.en: T (bearing an [acc] feature)

options.zh: Perf(完成體中心語)
options.en: Perf (the perfect head)

options.zh: C(標句詞)
options.en: C (the complementizer)

explanationZh: Adger 主張與 vP 相關的中心語帶 [acc];理論上 v 與 V 都可能,但下一節給出傾向性論證,認定是 little v 帶賓格,故 little v 與賓語 Agree 賦值為 [acc]。
explanationEn: Adger argues a head associated with vP bears [acc]; both v and V are candidates, but a suggestive argument in the next section favours little v, which Agrees with the object to value [acc].

questionZh: 為何「*Him has he known (him)」這種賓語提升到 Spec-TP 的推導被排除?Adger 提出的「配對局域性」(Locality of Matching)如何運作?
questionEn: Why is a derivation like '*Him has he known (him)', where the object raises to Spec-TP, ruled out? How does Adger's 'Locality of Matching' work?

options.zh: 主語介於 T 與賓語之間且也帶 [N],依配對局域性,T 的 EPP 只能與最近的(主語)配對,賓語無法越過主語與 T 配對
options.en: The subject intervenes between T and the object and also bears [N]; by Locality of Matching, T's EPP can only match the closest (the subject), so the object cannot skip over the subject to match T

options.zh: 賓語沒有格位特徵,故無法移位
options.en: The object has no case feature and so cannot move

options.zh: 賓語帶主格,與 EPP 不相容
options.en: The object bears nominative, which is incompatible with EPP

options.zh: T 不 c-command 賓語
options.en: T does not c-command the object

explanationZh: 配對局域性 (55):F on X 與 F on Y 之間若存在介入的 Z[F] 則不能 Agree。介入 (56):在 [X...Z...Y] 中,若 X c-command Z 且 Z c-command Y,則 Z 介入。主語 c-command 賓語、T c-command 主語,故主語介於兩者間並帶 [N],阻擋 EPP 與賓語配對;賓語永遠無法提升。
explanationEn: Locality of Matching (55): F on X cannot Agree with F on Y if an intervening Z[F] exists. Intervention (56): in [X...Z...Y], Z intervenes iff X c-commands Z and Z c-commands Y. The subject c-commands the object and T c-commands the subject, so the [N]-bearing subject intervenes and blocks EPP–object matching; the object can never raise.

questionZh: 在量詞滯留結構 [NP all [NP the dragons]] 中,為何較低的 NP「the dragons」能越過較高的 NP「all the dragons」移到 Spec-TP 而不違反配對局域性?
questionEn: In the stranding structure [NP all [NP the dragons]], why can the lower NP 'the dragons' move to Spec-TP over the higher NP 'all the dragons' without violating Locality of Matching?
answerTextZh: 因為在 [NP all [NP the dragons]] 中,兩個 NP 節點互不 c-command(較高 NP 並未 c-command 較低 NP),依介入定義 (56),較高 NP 不介於 T 與較低 NP 之間,故不阻擋配對,較低 NP 可被 EPP 提升。
answerTextEn: Because in [NP all [NP the dragons]] neither NP node c-commands the other (the higher NP does not c-command the lower NP); by the intervention definition (56), the higher NP does not intervene between T and the lower NP, so it does not block matching and the lower NP can be raised by EPP.
explanationZh: 介入需要 X c-command Z 且 Z c-command Y。較高 NP「all the dragons」並不 c-command 其內部的較低 NP「the dragons」,故不構成介入者,允許較低 NP 提升而把量詞 all 滯留下來。
explanationEn: Intervention requires X to c-command Z and Z to c-command Y. The higher NP 'all the dragons' does not c-command the contained lower NP 'the dragons', so it is not an intervener, allowing the lower NP to raise and stranding the quantifier all.

questionZh: 在 Adger 的分析中,主語-動詞一致(subject-verb agreement,如 -s)的資訊是如何傳遞到 little v 上被讀出的?
questionEn: In Adger's analysis, how is subject-verb agreement information (e.g. -s) transmitted to little v where it is pronounced?

options.zh: T 帶不可解讀的 φ 特徵,與主語的 φ 特徵 Agree 而取值,再經 Agree 把該值傳給 v 上的 [uInfl:]
options.en: T bears uninterpretable φ-features, Agrees with the subject's φ-features to get valued, then transmits that value to [uInfl:] on v via Agree

options.zh: 主語直接與 v Agree,不經過 T
options.en: The subject Agrees directly with v, bypassing T

options.zh: v 帶可解讀的 φ 特徵,不需任何一致關係
options.en: v bears interpretable φ-features and needs no agreement relation

options.zh: 一致透過賓語的格位特徵傳遞
options.en: Agreement is transmitted via the object's case feature

explanationZh: 現在式 T 帶 [uφ:];主語(如 the librarians[pl])的 [pl] 經 Agree 為 T 的 φ 賦值,T 再把已賦值的 φ 經 Agree 傳給 v 的 [uInfl:],使 v 讀出 -s 或零形式。一連串 Agree 把主語的一致資訊經 T 轉送到 v。
explanationEn: Present T bears [uφ:]; the subject's [pl] (e.g. the librarians[pl]) values T's φ under Agree, and T then transmits the valued φ to v's [uInfl:] via Agree, so v spells out -s or zero. A chain of Agree relays the subject's agreement through T onto v.

questionZh: 非賓格動詞(unaccusative,如 arrive、fall)的 little v 在特徵上有何特殊之處?
questionEn: What is special about the little v associated with unaccusative verbs (e.g. arrive, fall) in terms of its features?

options.zh: 它缺少賓格特徵 [acc],也缺少選擇性 uN 特徵(因而沒有指示語)
options.en: It lacks both the accusative feature [acc] and a selectional uN feature (and hence has no specifier)

options.zh: 它帶兩個 [acc] 特徵
options.en: It bears two [acc] features

options.zh: 它帶 [nom] 特徵取代 T
options.en: It bears [nom] in place of T

options.zh: 它帶強 EPP 特徵
options.en: It bears a strong EPP feature

explanationZh: 非賓格 little v 缺 [acc](故名 unaccusative)也缺 uN(故無指示語、無底層主語)。其唯一論元(Theme)為 V 的姊妹,可由 T 的 [nom] 賦主格,並因無介入主語而被 EPP 提升到 Spec-TP。
explanationEn: Unaccusative little v lacks [acc] (hence the name) and lacks uN (hence no specifier and no base subject). Its single Theme argument is sister of V, gets nominative from T's [nom], and—since no subject intervenes—is raised to Spec-TP by EPP.

questionZh: 非賓格動詞(如 arrive)的表面結構與非作格動詞(如 run)看起來相同,但推導不同:非賓格把底層________(賓語/主語)移到表面主語位置,非作格移的則是底層主語。
questionEn: An unaccusative verb (e.g. arrive) has a surface structure identical to an unergative one (e.g. run), but the derivations differ: the unaccusative moves an underlying ________ (object/subject) to surface subject position, whereas the unergative moves an underlying subject.
answerTextZh: 賓語(object;即 Theme 論元)
answerTextEn: object (the Theme argument)
explanationZh: Adger 明言:非賓格推導把底層賓語(Theme)移到表面主語位置,而非作格則移底層主語;表面雖相同,推導迥異。此預測:非賓格主語在某些現象中表現如賓語。
explanationEn: Adger states that the unaccusative derivation moves an underlying object (Theme) to surface subject position, while the unergative moves a base subject; the surfaces match but derivations differ. This predicts unaccusative subjects pattern with objects in certain phenomena.

questionZh: 義大利語的 ne-附著(ne-cliticization)如何支持「非賓格主語生成於賓語位置」的論點?
questionEn: How does Italian ne-cliticization support the claim that unaccusative subjects are generated in object position?

options.zh: ne-附著只能從賓語位置進行;它在非作格動詞(*Ne telefoneranno tre)不合語法,但在非賓格動詞(Ne arriveranno tre)合語法
options.en: ne-cliticization is only possible from object position; it is ungrammatical with an unergative (*Ne telefoneranno tre) but grammatical with an unaccusative (Ne arriveranno tre)

options.zh: ne-附著只能從主語位置進行
options.en: ne-cliticization is only possible from subject position

options.zh: ne-附著對非賓格與非作格沒有差別
options.en: ne-cliticization shows no contrast between unaccusatives and unergatives

options.zh: ne-附著需要強 EPP
options.en: ne-cliticization requires a strong EPP

explanationZh: 概括 (78):ne-附著只能從賓語位置進行。義大利語動詞提升到 T、主語可留原位。非作格主語在 Spec-vP,ne-附著不合語法;非賓格唯一論元在賓語位置,ne-附著合語法,故支持其賓語起源。
explanationEn: Generalization (78): ne-cliticization is only possible from object position. Italian raises the verb to T and lets the subject stay low. The unergative subject sits in Spec-vP so ne-cliticization fails; the unaccusative's single argument is in object position so it succeeds, supporting its object origin.

questionZh: 現代希伯來語的「領屬與格」(possessor dative,le-)如何區分非賓格與非作格動詞?請說明其概括 (89) 及其與 UTAH 的關聯。
questionEn: How do Modern Hebrew possessor datives (le-) distinguish unaccusative from unergative verbs? Explain generalization (89) and its link to the UTAH.
answerTextZh: 概括 (89):領屬與格無法與「(起源於) Spec-vP 的論元」連結。領屬與格不能指涉及物動詞主語、也不能指涉非作格主語(*Le-mi ha-xatulim yilelu,『誰的貓哀嚎』不合語法,因 whiners 是施事、在 Spec-vP);但可指涉非賓格主語(Le-mi ha-mitriya nafla,『誰的傘掉了』合語法,因 fall 的論元是 Theme、依 UTAH 為 V 的姊妹而非 Spec-vP)。此對比印證非賓格論元在賓語位置生成。
answerTextEn: Generalization (89): possessor datives cannot associate with arguments that originate in Spec-vP. They cannot mark the subject of a transitive or an unergative (*Le-mi ha-xatulim yilelu, 'whose cats whined', is ungrammatical because whiners are agents in Spec-vP), but they can mark an unaccusative subject (Le-mi ha-mitriya nafla, 'whose umbrella fell', is grammatical because the argument of fall is a Theme, sister of V by the UTAH, not in Spec-vP). The contrast confirms unaccusative arguments are generated in object position.
explanationZh: Adger 用此論證再次顯示非賓格/非作格之分:只有把非賓格唯一論元視為 V 的姊妹(賓語位置,非 Spec-vP),才能由 (89) 與 UTAH 共同正確地排除 (86) 並允許 (88)。
explanationEn: Adger uses this as a second argument for the unaccusative/unergative split: only by treating the unaccusative's single argument as sister of V (object position, not Spec-vP) do (89) and the UTAH jointly rule out (86) and admit (88).

questionZh: Adger 用功能中心語 Pass 來分析被動句。請說明 Pass 的範疇特徵、在英語中的實現、它選擇何種 little vP,以及這個選擇如何同時解釋被動句的兩個性質。
questionEn: Adger analyses passives with a functional head Pass. Explain Pass's categorial feature, its realization in English, what kind of little vP it selects, and how that selection simultaneously explains two properties of passives.
answerTextZh: Pass 帶範疇特徵 [Pass] 與 [uInfl:],在英語中由助動詞 be 實現,並選擇一個非賓格 little vP。因為所選的是非賓格 vP,該 vP 既不指派賓格、也沒有題元主語——這同時解釋了被動句缺賓格與缺題元主語兩個性質;於是賓語改與 T 核查 [nom] 並被 EPP 提升到 Spec-TP。Pass 帶強 Infl 特徵,像其他助動詞般提升到 T。
answerTextEn: Pass bears the categorial feature [Pass] plus [uInfl:], is realized in English by the auxiliary be, and selects an unaccusative little vP. Because the selected vP is unaccusative, it neither assigns accusative case nor has a thematic subject—this single choice explains both properties of passives; the object then checks [nom] with T and is raised to Spec-TP by EPP. Pass bears a strong Infl feature and raises to T like other auxiliaries.
explanationZh: Pass 是投射層級中的功能中心語,在英語由 be 實現,帶 [uInfl:],選擇非賓格 little vP。選非賓格 vP 同時解釋了被動句不指派賓格、也無題元主語;賓語因此核查 [nom] 並被 EPP 提升到 Spec-TP。
explanationEn: Pass is a functional head in the Hierarchy of Projections, realized as be in English, bears [uInfl:], and selects an unaccusative little vP. Selecting an unaccusative vP explains both the lack of accusative case and the absence of a thematic subject; the object thus checks [nom] and is raised to Spec-TP by EPP.

questionZh: Adger 指出英語被動句中主要動詞並非限定形式,而是________(過去分詞);Pass 為 little v 上的 [uInfl:] 賦值,其拼讀規則與帶完成體值的 v 相同。
questionEn: Adger notes that in an English passive the main verb is not finite but a ________ (past participle); Pass values [uInfl:] on little v, whose spellout rule is the same as for v bearing the perfect value.
answerTextZh: 過去分詞(past participle)
answerTextEn: past participle
explanationZh: 「The poisoned garments were stolen / *were stole」顯示被動主要動詞是過去分詞。Pass 帶 [uInfl:] 並像其他助動詞般提升到 T;它為 v 的 Infl 賦值,拼讀同帶完成體值的 v,故 be 之後接過去分詞。
explanationEn: 'The poisoned garments were stolen / *were stole' shows the passive main verb is a past participle. Pass bears [uInfl:] and raises to T like other auxiliaries; it values Infl on v with the same spellout as perfect-valued v, so be is followed by a past participle.

questionZh: 依本章,英語允許被動句以虛主語填補 Spec-TP 並讓賓語留在原位(如 *There were killed three men),這與法語相同。
questionEn: According to the chapter, English allows a passive with an expletive filling Spec-TP while the object stays in situ (e.g. *There were killed three men), just like French.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 為假。法語可以(Il a été tué trois hommes),但英語不行(*There were killed three men)。這正是 Adger 指出的被動跨語言變異:有些語言允許被動虛主語句,英語不允許。
explanationEn: False. French allows it (Il a été tué trois hommes) but English does not (*There were killed three men). This is exactly the cross-linguistic variation Adger notes: some languages allow expletive passives, English does not.

questionZh: 為何「Enkidu quickly freed the animals」這類句子支持「主語在 vP 之外」?
questionEn: Why does a sentence like 'Enkidu quickly freed the animals' support the claim that the subject is outside vP?

options.zh: 副詞 quickly 附加在 vP,卻出現在主語與動詞之間,故主語必定在 vP 之外(即 Spec-TP)
options.en: The adverb quickly adjoins to vP yet appears between the subject and the verb, so the subject must be outside vP (in Spec-TP)

options.zh: 副詞附加在 VP,故主語在 VP 內
options.en: The adverb adjoins to VP, so the subject is inside VP

options.zh: 副詞帶格位特徵把主語推到 vP 之外
options.en: The adverb bears case that pushes the subject out of vP

options.zh: 副詞是中心語,投射出主語位置
options.en: The adverb is a head projecting a subject position

explanationZh: manner 副詞附加在 vP。既然 quickly 介於主語與動詞之間,而它附在 vP,主語就必須在 vP 之上(Spec-TP)。TP 假設無需額外規定即可解釋此語序。
explanationEn: Manner adverbs adjoin to vP. Since quickly comes between the subject and the verb but adjoins to vP, the subject must be above vP (in Spec-TP). The TP hypothesis captures this with no extra stipulation.

questionZh: 「*Ron captured quickly a gryphon」在現代英語不合語法,但米爾頓 (1668) 的「Satan...undertakes alone the voyage」卻可以。Adger 如何解釋此歷時差異?
questionEn: '*Ron captured quickly a gryphon' is ungrammatical in modern English, yet Milton's (1668) 'Satan...undertakes alone the voyage' is fine. How does Adger explain this diachronic difference?

options.zh: 早期英語可把 V+v 提升到 T,使動詞越過 vP 附加副詞,故動詞與賓語間可插入副詞;後期英語禁止此提升
options.en: Earlier English could raise V+v to T, letting the verb cross a vP-adjoined adverb so an adverb can intervene between verb and object; later English barred this raising

options.zh: 早期英語的副詞附加在 VP 而非 vP
options.en: Earlier English adverbs adjoined to VP rather than vP

options.zh: 早期英語沒有 EPP 特徵
options.en: Earlier English lacked the EPP feature

options.zh: 早期英語的賓語帶主格
options.en: Earlier English objects bore nominative

explanationZh: 若動詞像法語般提升到 T,它會越過 vP 附加副詞,使副詞插在動詞與賓語之間合語法。1668 年英語允許主要動詞 V+v 提升到 T(如 undertakes alone the voyage、consents not),後期英語禁止此操作。
explanationEn: If the verb raises to T (as in French), it crosses a vP-adjoined adverb, making verb–adverb–object grammatical. In 1668 English allowed main-verb V+v raising to T (e.g. undertakes alone the voyage, consents not); later English barred it.

questionZh: Adger 如何用 EPP 特徵的參數化來分析 VSO 語言(如蘇格蘭蓋爾語)?
questionEn: How does Adger use parameterization of the EPP feature to analyse VSO languages such as Scottish Gaelic?

options.zh: VSO 語言中 T 的 EPP 特徵為弱(甚至缺失),故無物移到 Spec-TP,主語留在底層位置,而 v 中強的時制特徵把動詞提升到 T,得到 V-S-O 語序
options.en: In VSO languages the EPP on T is weak (or absent), so nothing moves to Spec-TP and the subject stays low, while strong tense on v raises the verb to T, giving V-S-O order

options.zh: VSO 語言中 EPP 特別強,把賓語也提升
options.en: In VSO languages EPP is especially strong, raising the object too

options.zh: VSO 語言沒有 little v
options.en: VSO languages have no little v

options.zh: VSO 語言的主語帶賓格
options.en: VSO subjects bear accusative case

explanationZh: 弱 EPP 表示沒有元素需要移到 Spec-TP,主語停留 Spec-vP;v 上強時制特徵把動詞複合體提升到 T,得到 V-S-O。VP(實為 vP)省略證據:回答 yes/no 問句時主語與賓語一同被刪,因兩者都在 vP 內。
explanationEn: A weak EPP means nothing must move to Spec-TP, so the subject stays in Spec-vP; strong tense on v raises the verbal complex to T, yielding V-S-O. Evidence from VP (really vP) ellipsis: in yes/no answers both subject and object delete because both are inside vP.

questionZh: 在 VSO 分析下,蘇格蘭蓋爾語回答 yes/no 問句時用重複動詞並對 vP 行省略,因主語與賓語都在 vP 內,故兩者一併被刪——這與英語只刪動詞和賓語形成對比。
questionEn: Under the VSO analysis, Scottish Gaelic answers a yes/no question by repeating the verb and ellipting vP; since both subject and object are inside vP, both are deleted—contrasting with English, where only the verb and object delete.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 英語主語已提升到 Spec-TP,故 vP 省略只刪動詞與賓語;VSO 語言主語留在 vP 內,省略 vP 時主語與賓語一起被刪(如 Chunnaic = 『I saw Mary』經 vP 省略後只剩動詞)。
explanationEn: In English the subject has raised to Spec-TP, so vP ellipsis deletes only verb and object; in a VSO language the subject stays inside vP, so ellipting vP deletes subject and object together (e.g. Chunnaic = 'I saw Mary' with vP ellipsis leaves only the verb).

questionZh: 依本章 §6.7 的類型學表 (131),寫出英語、法語、瑞典語、蘇格蘭蓋爾語在三個參數(助動詞時制、v 上時制、T 上 EPP)的強弱設定,並指出本章末提出的「缺漏可能性」問題。
questionEn: Per the typology table (131) in §6.7, state the strong/weak settings of the three parameters (tense on aux, tense on v, EPP on T) for English, French, Swedish, and Scottish Gaelic, and name the 'missing possibility' question Adger raises.
answerTextZh: 英語:aux 強、v 弱、EPP 強。法語:aux 強、v 強、EPP 強。瑞典語:aux 弱、v 弱、EPP 強。蘇格蘭蓋爾語:aux 強、v 強、EPP 弱。缺漏可能性:是否存在 EPP 弱且 aux/v 也弱的語言?——亦即一切都停留在 vP 內的語言;若此類語言不存在,為何如此?
answerTextEn: English: aux strong, v weak, EPP strong. French: aux strong, v strong, EPP strong. Swedish: aux weak, v weak, EPP strong. Scottish Gaelic: aux strong, v strong, EPP weak. Missing possibility: is there a language with weak EPP and also weak features on aux/v?—i.e. a language where everything stays inside vP; and if such languages do not exist, why not?
explanationZh: 此表把第 5 章的時制參數與本章新增的 EPP 參數整合,顯示跨語言變異源自特徵強弱的參數化。Adger 在 §6.7 結尾提出對稱性問題:為何沒有全部特徵皆弱的語言。
explanationEn: The table integrates the tense parameters from Chapter 5 with the new EPP parameter, showing cross-linguistic variation arises from parameterized feature strength. Adger ends §6.7 by raising the symmetry question of why no all-weak language seems to exist.

questionZh: 下列哪一組詞「不」屬於 Adger 所討論的 D 類（限定詞）次類別？
questionEn: Which of the following is NOT one of the subcategories of D (determiners) that Adger discusses?

options.zh: 冠詞（the, a）
options.en: Articles (the, a)

options.zh: 指示詞（this, that, these, those）
options.en: Demonstratives (this, that, these, those)

options.zh: 量詞（every, each, all, most）
options.en: Quantifiers (every, each, all, most)

options.zh: 情態助動詞（can, must, will）
options.en: Modal auxiliaries (can, must, will)

explanationZh: Adger 把 D 類詞分為三個傳統次類別：冠詞、指示詞與量詞。情態助動詞屬於範疇 T，不是 D。
explanationEn: Adger subclassifies D into three traditional types: articles, demonstratives, and quantifiers. Modal auxiliaries belong to category T, not D.

questionZh: Adger 用什麼判準把 the 和 a/this/that/some/every 歸為同一個範疇（D）？
questionEn: What criterion does Adger use to group the with a/this/that/some/every into a single category (D)?

options.zh: 它們都指派 θ-角色
options.en: They all assign θ-roles

options.zh: 它們是分布上的等價詞，能填入相同位置
options.en: They are distributional equivalents, slotting into the same position

options.zh: 它們都帶有複數特徵
options.en: They all bear a plural feature

options.zh: 它們都能單獨作為論元
options.en: They can all stand alone as arguments

explanationZh: the 有一組「分布上的等價詞」——能填入 phrase 中相同位置的詞。這個共同分布讓我們把它們歸為共享 D 特徵的一類。
explanationEn: the has a set of distributional equivalents—words that slot into the same position in the phrase. This shared distribution motivates grouping them under a shared D-feature.

questionZh: Adger 主張英語有一個空的複數限定詞，但沒有空的單數限定詞，因此 *letter is on the table 不合法，而 letters are on the table 合法。
questionEn: Adger argues English has a null plural determiner but no null singular determiner, which is why *letter is on the table is ungrammatical while letters are on the table is grammatical.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 光桿複數（bare plural）的合法性歸因於英語擁有一個空的複數限定詞；由於沒有空的單數限定詞，光桿單數不合法。限定詞在名詞片語中是強制性的。
explanationEn: The well-formedness of bare plurals is due to a null plural determiner in English; since there is no null singular determiner, bare singulars are ungrammatical. Determiners are obligatory in nominal phrases.

questionZh: 依 Adger 所述，區分近指（this/these）與遠指（that/those）的特徵與哪一種特徵相關？
questionEn: According to Adger, the feature distinguishing proximal (this/these) from distal (that/those) demonstratives is related to which feature?

options.zh: 數特徵（number）
options.en: Number features

options.zh: 人稱特徵（person）
options.en: Person features

options.zh: 格特徵（case）
options.en: Case features

options.zh: 性別特徵（gender）
options.en: Gender features

explanationZh: 近指與遠指的區別與人稱特徵相關：人稱特徵挑出說話者或受話者，近指類似地挑出靠近說話者的成分。蘇格蘭蓋爾語甚至有三分的指示詞系統。
explanationEn: The proximal/distal distinction relates to person features: person features pick out speaker or addressee, and proximal demonstratives similarly pick out an element near the speaker. Scottish Gaelic even has a three-way demonstrative system.

questionZh: 依 Adger 的語意分析，no vampire、every vampire 這類量化片語並不挑出指稱物（referent）。
questionEn: On Adger's semantic analysis, quantificational phrases like no vampire or every vampire do not pick out a referent.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 量化與定指不同：定指標示指稱物的言談屬性，而量化根本不挑出指稱物。no vampire 沒有指稱物，量詞的語意要在整句中以集合關係（不相交、包含）來理解。
explanationEn: Quantification differs from definiteness: definiteness signals a discourse property of a referent, whereas quantification picks out no referent at all. no vampire has no referent; quantifier semantics is understood via set relations (disjointness, inclusion) in whole sentences.

questionZh: 當某事物雖未被提及，但其存在可由先前話語推論而出、因此聽者能熟悉它時，仍可使用定冠詞；這種現象被稱為 ______。
questionEn: Using the definite article even when something has not been mentioned, but whose existence (and thus familiarity to the hearer) can be inferred from what has been said, is a phenomenon known as ______.
answerTextZh: 順應 / accommodation（容納）
answerTextEn: accommodation
explanationZh: 在「the cocktail shaker was really expensive」即使先前只說了 the party 時仍可接受，因為雞尾酒搖杯的存在可由派對推論出來。這種推論機制稱為 accommodation。
