explanationEn: In an exchange where one can say 'the cocktail shaker was really expensive' after only mentioning the party, the shaker's existence is inferable. This inference mechanism is called accommodation.

questionZh: Adger 用什麼最關鍵的語意論證來說明 D 是功能範疇（像 T 一樣），而非詞彙範疇？
questionEn: What is the key semantic argument Adger uses to show that D is a functional category (like T), not a lexical category?
answerTextZh: D 的語意「不是 θ-相關的」：限定詞不像 N 或 V 那樣指派 θ-角色給論元，而只是限制名詞所挑出的指稱範圍（涉及熟悉性、量化、近指等）。這種非 θ-相關的關係正與 T 對 vP 的關係相同,因此 D 與 T 一樣是功能範疇。
answerTextEn: D's semantics is 'non-θ-related': determiners do not assign θ-roles to arguments the way Ns or Vs do; they merely restrict the range of referents picked out by the nominal (involving familiarity, quantification, proximity). This non-θ-related relation parallels that between T and vP, so D, like T, is a functional category.
explanationZh: 全章的核心前提：D 的語意非 θ-相關,與 T、Neg、Perf 等功能範疇相同,因而支持把名詞片語重新分析為 DP。
explanationEn: This is the central premise of the chapter: D's non-θ-related semantics aligns it with functional categories like T, Neg, Perf, motivating the reanalysis of nominal phrases as DPs.

questionZh: *the this man、*each a man、*some those letters 這些例子說明了 D 類詞的什麼性質？
questionEn: Examples like *the this man, *each a man, *some those letters illustrate what property of Ds?

options.zh: 互補分布（complementary distribution）
options.en: Complementary distribution

options.zh: 一致關係（agreement）
options.en: Agreement

options.zh: θ-角色指派
options.en: θ-role assignment

options.zh: 中心語移位（head movement）
options.en: Head movement

explanationZh: Ds 處於互補分布：因為它們有完全相同的分布,所以競爭片語結構中的同一個位置,無法同時出現兩個。
explanationEn: Ds are in complementary distribution: because they have exactly the same distribution, they compete for the same position in phrase structure and two cannot co-occur.

questionZh: Adger 一開始提出的「名詞性投射階層（Nominal Hierarchy of Projections）」最簡形式為何？
questionEn: What is the simplest form of the Nominal Hierarchy of Projections that Adger first proposes?

options.zh: N > D
options.en: N > D

options.zh: D > N
options.en: D > N

options.zh: D > n > N
options.en: D > n > N

options.zh: D > Poss > n > N
options.en: D > Poss > n > N

explanationZh: 最初提出的名詞性階層只是 D > N,與小句階層 T > ... > v > V 平行。後續章節才逐步擴充為 D > (Poss) > n > N。
explanationEn: The initially proposed nominal hierarchy is just D > N, paralleling the clausal hierarchy. Later sections elaborate it to D > (Poss) > n > N.

questionZh: 採用 DP 假設後,動詞、介系詞與 T 的 EPP 特徵的選擇特徵都必須由 [uN] 重新分類為 [uD]（例如 EPP 變成 [uD*]）。
questionEn: Once the DP Hypothesis is adopted, the selectional features of verbs, prepositions, and T's EPP feature must be recategorized from [uN] to [uD] (e.g. EPP becomes [uD*]).

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 由於名詞片語現在是 DP,所有原本選擇 N 的成分（V、P、帶 EPP 的 T）都改為選擇 D。EPP 特徵因此從 [uN*] 改為 [uD*]。
explanationEn: Since nominal phrases are now DPs, all elements that selected N (V, P, and T with EPP) instead select D. The EPP feature thus changes from [uN*] to [uD*].

questionZh: 在 DP 假設下,為什麼 I wrote letters 中的光桿名詞 letters 必須是 DP 而非單純的 NP？
questionEn: Under the DP Hypothesis, why must the bare noun letters in I wrote letters be a DP rather than a plain NP?

options.zh: 因為 letters 帶有複數特徵
options.en: Because letters bears a plural feature

options.zh: 因為動詞 write c-選擇一個 DP,如 I wrote the letters 所示
options.en: Because the verb write c-selects a DP, as shown by I wrote the letters

options.zh: 因為 letters 出現在主語位置
options.en: Because letters appears in subject position

options.zh: 因為 letters 不能被形容詞修飾
options.en: Because letters cannot be modified by an adjective

explanationZh: write c-選擇 DP（見 I wrote the letters）。因此 I wrote letters 中的 letters 也必須是 DP——一個帶空 D 的 DP,以滿足動詞的 c-選擇要求。法語等語言因為沒有可用的空 D,光桿名詞不合法。
explanationEn: write c-selects a DP (cf. I wrote the letters). So letters in I wrote letters must also be a DP—one headed by a null D—to satisfy the verb's c-selection. In languages like French, no null D is available, so bare nouns are ungrammatical.

questionZh: Adger 如何把代名詞（如 these, him）納入 DP 系統？
questionEn: How does Adger incorporate pronouns (e.g. these, him) into the DP system?

options.zh: 把它們當作帶空 D 的 NP
options.en: As NPs headed by a null D

options.zh: 把它們直接當作 D
options.en: As Ds themselves

options.zh: 把它們當作 little n
options.en: As little n

options.zh: 把它們當作 PP 的補語
options.en: As complements of PP

explanationZh: 代名詞被直接當作 D。當代名詞被合併進樹中,它獲得 θ-角色並被選擇,因而自動具有片語地位。這也解釋了 We linguists、You friends of the king 這類代名詞例外地帶 NP 補語的例子。
explanationEn: Pronouns are treated simply as Ds. When merged, a pronoun receives a θ-role and is selected, automatically gaining phrasal status. This also explains [We linguists] and [You friends of the king], where a pronoun-D exceptionally takes an NP complement.

questionZh: Adger 主張 Saxon genitive（如 Evan's idea 中的 Evan's）位於 DP 結構中的哪個位置？
questionEn: Where does Adger argue the Saxon genitive (e.g. Evan's in Evan's idea) sits in DP structure?

options.zh: D 中心語本身
options.en: The D head itself

options.zh: 一個空 D 的指示語（Spec-DP）
options.en: The specifier of a null D (Spec-DP)

options.zh: NP 的補語
options.en: The complement of NP

options.zh: little n 的中心語
options.en: The head of little n

explanationZh: Saxon genitive 顯然可以是 XP（如 [One of our oldest friend's] idea）,所以不能是 X 等級的 D 中心語。正確分析是把它放在一個空 D 的指示語。every 與 Saxon genitive 不互補分布（Evan's every idea）正支持這個多出來的結構空間。
explanationEn: Saxon genitives can clearly be XPs (e.g. [One of our oldest friend's] idea), so they cannot occupy the X-level D head. The correct analysis places them in the specifier of a null D. That every is not in complementary distribution with the Saxon genitive (Evan's every idea) supports this extra structural space.

questionZh: 因為 *The Evan's idea 不合法,Adger 結論說 Saxon genitive 一定與所有限定詞互補分布。
questionEn: Because *The Evan's idea is ungrammatical, Adger concludes that the Saxon genitive must be in complementary distribution with all determiners.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 假。Adger 明確指出 every 是一個「不」與 Saxon genitive 互補分布的明顯外露限定詞（Evan's every idea、The Emperor's every wish）。因此不能說 Saxon genitive 與所有 D 互補,而要把它放在空 D 的指示語。
explanationEn: False. Adger explicitly notes that every is an overt determiner that is NOT in complementary distribution with the Saxon genitive (Evan's every idea; The Emperor's every wish). So one cannot say it is in complementary distribution with all Ds; instead it sits in Spec of a null D.

questionZh: 在 §7.2 中,Adger 共辨識出英語裡四種空 D,分別與下列名詞或結構同現:複數名詞、______名詞、帶屬格指示語(Saxon genitive),以及______名詞(proper names)。
questionEn: In §7.2 Adger identifies four null Ds in English, occurring with: plural nouns, ______ nouns, a genitive specifier (Saxon genitive), and ______ names.
answerTextZh: 不可數/物質(mass);專有(proper)
answerTextEn: mass; proper
explanationZh: 四種空 D:複數空 D、物質名詞空 D(帶 [umass]、與物質名詞一致)、屬格空 D(帶 Saxon genitive 指示語、檢核 [gen]),以及專有名詞空 D(帶 [uproper])。
explanationEn: The four null Ds are: a plural null D; a mass null D (bearing [umass], agreeing with a mass noun); a genitive null D (with a Saxon genitive specifier, checking [gen]); and a proper-name null D (bearing [uproper]).

questionZh: Adger 如何用拼讀(spellout)機制解釋為何「*man arrived」不合法,而「Men arrived」合法?
questionEn: How does Adger use the spellout mechanism to explain why *man arrived is bad but Men arrived is fine?

options.zh: 空 D 帶不可解讀的 [unumber],由 N 的數特徵核值;帶複數值的不定 D 拼讀為空,帶單數值的拼讀為 a,故沒有空的單數不定 D
options.en: The null/indefinite D has [unumber] valued by N; the plural-valued indefinite D spells out as null, the singular-valued one as a, so there is no null singular indefinite D

options.zh: 單數名詞無法移入 Spec-TP
options.en: Singular nouns cannot move to Spec-TP

options.zh: 單數名詞缺少 θ-角色
options.en: Singular nouns lack a θ-role

options.zh: T 對單數名詞不指派格
options.en: T fails to assign case to singular nouns

explanationZh: 不定限定詞帶 [unum: ]。當它與 N(P) 合併,N 的可解讀數特徵核值它。拼讀規則讓帶複數值的 D 得到空語音,帶單數值的 D 得到 a。因此單數不定 D 不可能是空的,*man 被排除。
explanationEn: The indefinite determiner bears [unum: ]; when it merges with N(P), N's interpretable number feature values it. Spellout gives the plural-valued D a null pronunciation and the singular-valued D the pronunciation a. Hence a singular indefinite D can never be null, ruling out *man.

questionZh: 在 DP 假設下,Adger 如何重新分析浮游量詞(floating quantifier),又這帶來什麼新問題?
questionEn: Under the DP Hypothesis, how does Adger reanalyse floating quantifiers, and what new problem does this raise?
answerTextZh: 浮游量詞被分析為帶 DP 補語的 D,即 [Q[D] [DP ...]]。這解決了上一章「量詞為何不投射」的問題——它們現在確實投射,且因取 DP 補語,當補語為滿足 EPP 而移至 Spec-TP 時量詞可被遺留(stranded)。新問題是:這個結構有兩個 D 節點,並不嚴格符合投射階層。
answerTextEn: Floating quantifiers are reanalysed as Ds taking a DP complement: [Q[D] [DP ...]]. This solves the previous chapter's problem of why quantifiers fail to project—they now do project, and because they take a DP complement, the complement can raise to Spec-TP for EPP, stranding the quantifier. The new problem: this structure has two D nodes and does not conform strictly to the Hierarchy of Projections.
explanationZh: 把浮游量詞當作取 DP 補語的 D 可解釋 stranding,但兩個 D 節點違反階層,Adger 承認此處留有未解的特殊性。
explanationEn: Treating the floating quantifier as a D with a DP complement explains stranding, but the two D nodes violate the hierarchy; Adger leaves this special property unsolved.

questionZh: 在 DP 內,當同時有 Agent 與 Theme 兩個論元時(如 the therapist's analysis of Morticia),它們各以什麼方式實現?
questionEn: Within a DP containing both an Agent and a Theme (e.g. the therapist's analysis of Morticia), how is each realized?

options.zh: Agent 為 Saxon genitive,Theme 為 of-PP
options.en: Agent as Saxon genitive, Theme as an of-PP

options.zh: Agent 為 of-PP,Theme 為 Saxon genitive
options.en: Agent as an of-PP, Theme as a Saxon genitive

options.zh: 兩者皆為 of-PP
options.en: Both as of-PPs

options.zh: 兩者皆為 Saxon genitive
options.en: Both as Saxon genitives

explanationZh: Adger 的概括:Agent 只能實現為 Saxon genitive(不能是 of-PP);Theme 兩者皆可。當兩者同現時,Agent 必為 Saxon genitive,Theme 必為 of-PP。
explanationEn: Adger's generalization: the Agent can only be a Saxon genitive (never an of-PP); the Theme can be either. When both appear, the Agent must be the Saxon genitive and the Theme must be the of-PP.

questionZh: Adger 指出,在小句中論元帶主格與賓格,但在 DP 內找不到主格與賓格,取而代之的是屬格(genitive)與 of-PP。
questionEn: Adger notes that in a clause arguments bear nominative and accusative, but inside a DP nominative and accusative are impossible; instead one finds genitive and an of-PP.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 對比 She analysed her(主格/賓格)與 *He analysis her vs His analysis of her。DP 內不容主格賓格,論元以屬格與 of-PP 實現。
explanationEn: Compare She analysed her (nom/acc) with *He analysis her vs His analysis of her. DPs do not license nom/acc; arguments surface as genitive and of-PP.

questionZh: Adger 引入 little n 的主要理論動機是什麼?
questionEn: What is Adger's main theoretical motivation for introducing little n?

options.zh: 為了解釋限定詞的互補分布
options.en: To explain the complementary distribution of determiners

options.zh: 為了把 UTAH 推廣到名詞域:Agent 在 Spec-n 生成,Theme 在 NP 之下,正如 vP 中的情形
options.en: To extend the UTAH to the nominal domain: Agents are merged in Spec-n, Themes as daughters of NP, just as in vP

options.zh: 為了提供 Saxon genitive 的落腳位置
options.en: To provide a landing site for the Saxon genitive

options.zh: 為了解釋指示詞的近指/遠指對比
options.en: To explain the proximal/distal contrast of demonstratives

explanationZh: 正如 VP 包在 little v 中,NP 包在 little n 中。把 UTAH 推廣到名詞域:Theme 為 NP 的女兒,Agent 在 Spec-n。這解釋了為何 Agent 不能作為後置 of-PP(*the imposition of the government of a fine)。
explanationEn: Just as VP is contained in little v, NP is contained in little n. Generalizing the UTAH to nominals: Themes are daughters of NP, Agents are in Spec-n. This explains why Agents cannot be post-nominal of-PPs (*the imposition of the government of a fine).

questionZh: Adger 用哪兩類證據來論證 Agent 在名詞片語中合併於 Spec-n(平行於 vP)?
questionEn: Which two kinds of evidence does Adger use to argue that Agents in noun phrases are merged in Spec-n (parallel to vP)?

options.zh: 格指派與一致
options.en: Case assignment and agreement

options.zh: 約束(reflexive binding)與並列(co-ordination)
options.en: Reflexive binding and co-ordination

options.zh: wh-移位與島嶼效應
options.en: Wh-movement and island effects

options.zh: 拼讀規則與音韻
options.en: Spellout rules and phonology

explanationZh: 證據一:約束。the consul's gift of the gladiator to himself 合法,*...of himself to the gladiator 不合法,顯示 Theme c-命令 Goal,如 vP。證據二:並列。Richard's gift [of the helicopter to the hospital] and [of the bus to the school] 顯示 little n 投射可被並列。
explanationEn: Evidence 1: binding. the consul's gift of the gladiator to himself is good but *...of himself to the gladiator is bad, showing the Theme c-commands the Goal, as in vP. Evidence 2: co-ordination. Richard's gift [of the helicopter to the hospital] and [of the bus to the school] shows the little-n projection can be co-ordinated.

questionZh: 依 Adger,little n 只在 DP 含有 Agent 時才投射;若名詞沒有 Agent(如 cat),則沒有 n 投射。
questionEn: According to Adger, little n projects only when a DP contains an Agent; if a noun has no Agent (like cat), there is no n projection.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 假。little n 是強制性投射(像 little v),只是可以沒有指示語。即使是 cat 或無 Agent 的 gift,名詞仍須提升到 n 以得到正確語序(N 在 Theme、Goal 之前),所以 n 一定投射。
explanationEn: False. Little n is an obligatory projection (like little v); it may simply lack a specifier. Even cat or an Agent-less gift requires N to raise to n for correct word order (N before Theme and Goal), so n is always projected.

questionZh: 在 DP 內的格核查中,little n 對 of-Phrase 核查的弱格特徵記為 [______],而空 D 對 Agent 核查的強格特徵記為 [______*]。
questionEn: In DP-internal case checking, little n checks a weak case feature on the of-Phrase written [______], while the null D checks a strong case feature on the Agent written [______*].
answerTextZh: of;gen
answerTextEn: of; gen
explanationZh: n 帶弱的 [of] 特徵,故 of-Phrase 留在原位;D 帶強的 [gen*] 特徵,與 Agent(或 Possessor、Theme)的格特徵 Agree 並將最近的 DP 吸引到 Spec-DP(如 Evan's every desire 中 Evan's 提升至 D 之上)。
explanationEn: n bears a weak [of] feature, so the of-Phrase stays in situ; D bears a strong [gen*] feature, which Agrees with the case feature of the Agent (or Possessor/Theme) and attracts the closest DP to Spec-DP (e.g. Evan's raising past every in Evan's every desire).

questionZh: Adger 如何解釋 the enemy's destruction 中 Theme 竟能以 Saxon genitive 出現?這與小句中的什麼結構平行?
questionEn: How does Adger explain that in the enemy's destruction the Theme can surface as a Saxon genitive? What clausal structure does this parallel?
answerTextZh: 因為 little n 只「選擇性地」投射指示語(其選擇 D 特徵是可選的)。當 little n 不投射指示語(無 Agent)時,D 的強 [gen*] 特徵就與生成為 Theme 的 DP(the enemy)之格特徵相配,把 Theme 吸引到 Spec-DP 並實現為屬格。其衍生平行於非賓格(unaccusative)動詞:Theme 在缺乏外論元時提升到較高位置。
answerTextEn: Because little n only optionally projects a specifier (its selectional D-feature is optional). When little n projects no specifier (no Agent), D's strong [gen*] feature matches the case feature of the DP generated as Theme (the enemy), attracting it to Spec-DP and realizing it as genitive. The derivation parallels an unaccusative: the Theme raises to a higher position in the absence of an external argument.
explanationZh: Theme-as-genitive 衍生與非賓格主語平行——皆涉及 Theme 在無外論元時向上移動,由上方功能中心語核值格。
explanationEn: The Theme-as-genitive derivation parallels unaccusative subjects—both involve the Theme moving up in the absence of an external argument, with case valued by a higher functional head.

questionZh: 對於像 Jenny's cat、Anson's book 這類真正的領屬者(possessor),Adger 提議它們在何處合併?
questionEn: For true possessors like Jenny's cat or Anson's book, where does Adger propose they are merged?

options.zh: Spec-n
options.en: Spec-n

options.zh: NP 的女兒(daughter of NP)
options.en: Daughter of NP

options.zh: 一個可選功能中心語 Poss 的指示語(Spec-PossP)
options.en: The specifier of an optional functional head Poss (Spec-PossP)

options.zh: D 中心語本身
options.en: The D head itself

explanationZh: 領屬者不能合併於 NP 女兒或 Spec-n(這些位置保留給特定 θ-角色)。Adger 提議一個可選功能中心語 Poss,領屬者合併於其指示語;Poss 在其指示語與 nP 之間建立一個不確定的語意關係(常為領屬,但不必然)。其後領屬者的 [case] 由 D 的 [gen] 核查並移至 Spec-DP。
explanationEn: Possessors cannot merge as daughters of NP or in Spec-n (reserved for specific θ-roles). Adger posits an optional functional head Poss, with the possessor in its specifier; Poss establishes an underdetermined semantic relation between its specifier and nP (often possession, but not necessarily). The possessor's [case] is then checked by D's [gen] and it raises to Spec-DP.

questionZh: Adger 對 DP 中 AP 修飾語(如 Mary's striking resemblance to Sue)採取什麼分析?
questionEn: What analysis does Adger adopt for AP modifiers within DP (e.g. Mary's striking resemblance to Sue)?

options.zh: AP 是功能中心語的指示語
options.en: APs are specifiers of a functional head

options.zh: AP 附加(adjoin)於 nP,如同副詞附加於 vP
options.en: APs adjoin to nP, just as adverbs adjoin to vP

options.zh: AP 是 D 的補語
options.en: APs are complements of D

options.zh: AP 被指派 θ-角色,合併於 NP 之下
options.en: APs are assigned θ-roles and merge below NP

explanationZh: Adger 採取附加分析,維持對附加語處理的一致性:AP 附加於 nP(如副詞附加於 vP)。這預測 AP 是可選的(Adjoin 不由特徵核查觸發),且可遞迴(輸出仍是 nP)——兩項預測皆獲確認(Jenny's scraggy extremely demonic cat)。
explanationEn: Adger adopts the adjunction analysis for uniform treatment of adjuncts: APs adjoin to nP (as adverbs adjoin to vP). This predicts APs are optional (Adjoin is not feature-triggered) and recursive (the output is still nP)—both confirmed (Jenny's scraggy extremely demonic cat).

questionZh: Adger 如何用特徵強度的參數變異來分析希伯來語的 construct state(如 harisat ha-oyev 'et ha-ir)?
questionEn: How does Adger analyse the Hebrew construct state (e.g. harisat ha-oyev 'et ha-ir) via parametric variation in feature strength?

options.zh: [gen] 強而 [unum] 弱,故 Agent 提升至 Spec-DP
options.en: [gen] is strong and [unum] is weak, so the Agent raises to Spec-DP

options.zh: [unum] 強而 [gen] 弱,故 N+n 複合體提升到 D(填滿 D、無外露限定詞),Agent 留在原位於名詞之後
options.en: [unum] is strong and [gen] is weak, so the N+n complex raises to D (filling D, no overt determiner) and the Agent stays in situ after the noun

options.zh: [gen] 與 [unum] 皆強,故名詞與 Agent 都移位
options.en: Both [gen] and [unum] are strong, so both the noun and the Agent move

options.zh: [gen] 與 [unum] 皆弱,故沒有任何移位
options.en: Both [gen] and [unum] are weak, so nothing moves

explanationZh: 與英語相反,希伯來語 D 上的 [unum] 為強而 [gen] 為弱。強 [unum] 觸發 N+n 複合體提升到 D,故沒有空間放外露限定詞(*ha-harisat...);弱 [gen] 使 Agent 留在名詞之後;UTAH 則確保 Agent–Theme 的嚴格語序。
explanationEn: Unlike English, in Hebrew [unum] on D is strong and [gen] is weak. Strong [unum] triggers raising of the N+n complex to D, so there is no room for an overt determiner (*ha-harisat...); weak [gen] leaves the Agent after the noun; the UTAH fixes the strict Agent–Theme order.

questionZh: 在第七章摘要中,Adger 給出的完整名詞性投射階層為:D > (______) > ______ > N。
questionEn: In the Chapter 7 summary, Adger gives the full nominal Hierarchy of Projections as: D > (______) > ______ > N.
answerTextZh: Poss;n
answerTextEn: Poss; n
explanationZh: 最終的名詞性階層為 D > (Poss) > n > N,與小句階層 T > (Neg) > (Perf) > (Prog) > (Pass) > v > V 平行。Poss 為可選功能中心語(故加括號),n 為強制投射。
explanationEn: The final nominal hierarchy is D > (Poss) > n > N, paralleling the clausal hierarchy T > (Neg) > (Perf) > (Prog) > (Pass) > v > V. Poss is an optional functional head (hence parenthesized); n is obligatory.

questionZh: 在「I claimed that she was pregnant」中,引介內嵌子句的詞 that 屬於哪一個句法範疇?
questionEn: In "I claimed that she was pregnant", the word that introducing the embedded clause belongs to which syntactic category?

options.zh: C(標句詞)
options.en: C (Complementizer)

options.zh: D(限定詞)
options.en: D (Determiner)

options.zh: T(時制)
options.en: T (Tense)

options.zh: V(動詞)
options.en: V (Verb)

explanationZh: Adger 主張 that 與其他標句詞屬於新範疇 C(Complementizer)。它與 TP 合併並投射成 CP,而非與限定詞系統的 that 同類。
explanationEn: Adger argues that and other complementizers belong to the new category C (Complementizer). It merges with TP and projects a CP, and is not the same as the demonstrative that of the determiner system.

questionZh: 下列哪一項證據顯示 that 與其後的 TP 形成一個成分(constituent),而非與前面的動詞結合?
questionEn: Which piece of evidence shows that that forms a constituent with the following TP, rather than with the preceding verb?

options.zh: 假分裂句(pseudoclefting)可把 that+TP 一起移到 was 之後
options.en: Pseudoclefting can move that+TP together to after was

options.zh: that 永遠不能省略
options.en: that can never be omitted

options.zh: that 指派 θ-角色給主語
options.en: that assigns a θ-role to the subject

options.zh: that 必須出現在動詞之前
options.en: that must appear before the verb

explanationZh: 假分裂句如「What she thought was that the poison was neutralized」顯示 that 與 TP 一起被移動,且 that 不能被遺留(stranded),證明兩者成分。
explanationEn: Pseudoclefts like "What she thought was that the poison was neutralized" show that moves together with the TP and cannot be stranded, proving they form a constituent.

questionZh: 在某些語言(如蓋爾語 Gaelic)中,所有子句補語都必須使用標句詞,不像英語的 that 可以選擇性省略。
questionEn: In some languages (e.g. Gaelic), the complementizer is obligatory for all clausal complements, unlike English that which can be optionally omitted.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 如蓋爾語 (12)–(13) 與法語 (14)–(15) 所示,這些語言的標句詞 (gu/qu') 對所有子句補語都是強制的,英語的 that 則常可省略。
explanationEn: As Gaelic (12)-(13) and French (14)-(15) show, the complementizer (gu/qu') is obligatory for all clausal complements in those languages, whereas English that is often optional.

questionZh: 請舉出 Adger 用來反對「把標句詞 that 分析為限定詞 D」的兩個論證。
questionEn: Give two arguments Adger uses against analysing the complementizer that as a determiner (D).
answerTextZh: (1) 其他指示詞(如 this、those)不能出現在這個位置並得到相同解讀(如 *I said this he left);(2) 其他語言的標句詞(法語 que、蓋爾語 gu)與其指示詞系統(ce/cela;seo/sin)完全不同;(3) 英語還有其他標句詞(如 whether、if、for)與限定詞系統毫無相似之處。
answerTextEn: (1) Other demonstratives (this, those) cannot occur in this position with the relevant reading (*I said this he left); (2) other languages have complementizers (French que, Gaelic gu) totally unlike their demonstrative systems (ce/cela; seo/sin); (3) English has other complementizers (whether, if, for) bearing no resemblance to the determiner system.
explanationZh: Adger 給出三個論證,任答兩個即可:指示詞替換失敗、跨語言證據、英語其他標句詞的存在。
explanationEn: Adger gives three arguments; any two suffice: failure of demonstrative substitution, cross-linguistic evidence, and the existence of other English complementizers.

questionZh: 依 Adger 的分析,描述子句是疑問或陳述的特徵稱為什麼?
questionEn: According to Adger, the feature describing whether a clause is a question or a statement is called what?

options.zh: [clause-type],值為 [Q] 或 [Decl]
options.en: [clause-type], valued [Q] or [Decl]

options.zh: [case],值為 [nom] 或 [acc]
options.en: [case], valued [nom] or [acc]

options.zh: [EPP],值為 [strong] 或 [weak]
options.en: [EPP], valued [strong] or [weak]

options.zh: [tense],值為 [past] 或 [pres]
options.en: [tense], valued [past] or [pres]

explanationZh: Adger 假設一個 [clause-type] 特徵,值為 [Q](疑問)或 [Decl](陳述)。此特徵在 C 上是可解讀的,產生陳述句與疑問句不同的語意。
explanationEn: Adger posits a [clause-type] feature valued either [Q] (interrogative) or [Decl] (declarative). This feature is interpretable on C and gives rise to the different semantics of declaratives and questions.

questionZh: 英語中引介疑問子句(interrogative)、且與 that 呈互補分布的兩個標句詞是 ________ 與 ________。
questionEn: The two English complementizers that introduce interrogative clauses and are in complementary distribution with that are ________ and ________.
answerTextZh: whether 與 if
answerTextEn: whether and if
explanationZh: whether 與 if 引介疑問子句,與 that 互補分布(*asked whether that...),且與其後子句成分,故也是標句詞,但帶 [Q] 而非 [Decl]。
explanationEn: whether and if introduce interrogative clauses, are in complementary distribution with that (*asked whether that...), and form constituents with their clause, so they too are complementizers, but bear [Q] rather than [Decl].

questionZh: Adger 主張 C 是功能範疇而非詞彙範疇。他給的關鍵理由是什麼?
questionEn: Adger argues that C is a functional rather than a lexical category. What is the key reason he gives?
answerTextZh: 因為 C 不指派 θ-角色。標句詞的語意功能只是指示聽者如何看待整個命題(視為陳述事實或對事實的提問),並不像詞彙範疇那樣賦予論元題旨角色。
answerTextEn: Because C does not assign a θ-role. The semantic function of a complementizer is merely to indicate how the hearer should think of the whole proposition (as a statement of fact or a question about the facts), not to assign thematic roles to arguments as lexical categories do.
explanationZh: Adger 明確指出 C 必須是功能範疇而非詞彙範疇,因為它不指派 θ-角色;標句詞的語意功能是指示聽者如何看待整個命題。
explanationEn: Adger states C must be functional rather than lexical because it does not assign a θ-role; the semantic function of complementizers is to indicate how the hearer should think of the whole proposition.

questionZh: 依 Adger 對主語-助動詞倒裝(SAI)的分析,是什麼觸發了 T 移位到 C?
questionEn: In Adger's analysis of Subject-Auxiliary Inversion (SAI), what triggers movement of T to C?

options.zh: C[Q] 把 T 上不可解讀的 [uclause-type] 特徵賦值為強的 Q,而強特徵觸發移位
options.en: C[Q] values the uninterpretable [uclause-type] feature on T as strong Q, and a strong feature triggers movement

options.zh: T 上的 EPP 特徵吸引主語
options.en: The EPP feature on T attracts the subject

options.zh: v 上的 [acc] 特徵核查賓語
options.en: The [acc] feature on v checks the object

options.zh: C 指派 θ-角色給 T
options.en: C assigns a θ-role to T

explanationZh: T 帶不可解讀、未賦值的 [uclause-type: ] 特徵,當 C[Q] 合併時把它賦值為強 Q (Q*),強特徵迫使 T(連同其內所有東西)移入 C,造成倒裝。
explanationEn: T bears an uninterpretable unvalued [uclause-type: ] feature; when C[Q] merges it values it as strong Q (Q*), and the strong feature forces T (with everything in it) to move into C, producing inversion.

questionZh: Adger 的 T-to-C 分析如何同時預測 yes/no 問句中的 do-support(如 Did the potion boil over?)?
questionEn: How does Adger's T-to-C analysis also predict do-support in yes/no questions (e.g. Did the potion boil over?)?

options.zh: T 移到 C 後不再成分統御 little v,無法形成鏈結,故時制無法被發音,do-support 作為最後手段插入
options.en: After T moves to C it no longer c-commands little v, so no chain can form, tense cannot be pronounced, and do-support applies as a last resort

options.zh: do 本身帶 [Q] 特徵,所以直接合併在 C
options.en: do itself bears a [Q] feature and merges directly in C

options.zh: do 指派 accusative 給主語
options.en: do assigns accusative to the subject

options.zh: C 強制主語下降到 T
options.en: C forces the subject to lower into T

explanationZh: 疑問 C 觸發 T 移到 C 後,T 不再成分統御 little v,二者間無法形成鏈結,發音時制的規則失效,故 do-support 作為最後手段適用。
explanationEn: Interrogative C triggers T-to-C movement; afterwards T no longer c-commands little v, so no chain forms, the Rule for Pronouncing Tense fails, and do-support applies as a last resort.

questionZh: 依 Adger 的分析,英語陳述主句的 [Decl] 值在 T 上是強的,因此也會觸發 T 移到 C。
questionEn: In Adger's analysis, the [Decl] value on T in English declarative matrix clauses is strong, so it also triggers T-to-C movement.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 錯誤。在英語中 Q 值是強的(觸發 SAI),但 Decl 值是弱的,故陳述句不發生移位。Adger 也說英語的 [Decl] 句法效果微不足道。
explanationEn: False. In English the Q value is strong (triggering SAI) but the Decl value is weak, so no movement occurs in declaratives. Adger notes [Decl] has trivial syntactic effects in English.

questionZh: yes/no 問句(如 Did the magic work?)中助動詞出現在主語之前的傳統名稱是 ________(英文縮寫亦可)。
questionEn: The traditional name for the construction in yes/no questions where the auxiliary appears before the subject (e.g. Did the magic work?) is ________ (abbreviation acceptable).
answerTextZh: 主語-助動詞倒裝(Subject-Auxiliary Inversion,SAI)
answerTextEn: Subject-Auxiliary Inversion (SAI)
explanationZh: 第一個助動詞與主語倒裝,稱為 Subject-Auxiliary Inversion (SAI);Adger 分析為 T 移到 C。
explanationEn: The first auxiliary inverts with the subject; this is Subject-Auxiliary Inversion (SAI), analysed by Adger as T-to-C movement.

questionZh: Adger 採用的「主語子句」(initial CP,如 [That Jason arrived] infuriated Medea)分析是哪一種?
questionEn: Which analysis does Adger adopt for subject clauses (initial CPs, e.g. [That Jason arrived] infuriated Medea)?

options.zh: initial CP 在一個特殊 C 的指示語位置,真正的主語位置由一個空代詞填充
options.en: The initial CP sits in the specifier of a special C, with a null pronoun filling the true subject position

options.zh: initial CP 直接在 TP 的指示語(標準主語位置)
options.en: The initial CP is directly in the specifier of TP (standard subject position)

options.zh: initial CP 是動詞的賓語,經被動移到句首
options.en: The initial CP is the object, moved to front by passive

options.zh: initial CP 是附加語,附加到 vP
options.en: The initial CP is an adjunct, adjoined to vP

explanationZh: Adger 採用 specifier-of-CP 分析:initial CP 在特殊 C[uC] 的指示語,空代詞(英語為零形式,荷蘭語為 dat)在 TP 指示語的真正主語位置。
explanationEn: Adger adopts the specifier-of-CP analysis: the initial CP sits in the specifier of a special C[uC], with a null pronoun (null in English, dat in Dutch) in the true subject position in spec-TP.

questionZh: 下列哪一項是支持「initial CP 不在一般主語位置」的證據?
questionEn: Which of the following supports the claim that initial CPs are not in the ordinary subject position?

options.zh: initial CP 不接受 SAI(*Did that Medea killed her children upset Jason?),且荷蘭語中還出現特殊代詞 dat
options.en: Initial CPs do not undergo SAI (*Did that Medea killed her children upset Jason?), and Dutch shows a special pronoun dat

options.zh: initial CP 永遠帶 accusative 格
options.en: Initial CPs always bear accusative case

options.zh: initial CP 必須含 PRO
options.en: Initial CPs must contain PRO

options.zh: initial CP 只能出現在非限定子句
options.en: Initial CPs can only appear in non-finite clauses

explanationZh: 證據包括:initial CP 不接受 SAI、不能套在另一個 initial CP 內,以及荷蘭語出現特殊代詞 dat,均顯示 initial CP 與一般 DP 主語位置不同。
explanationEn: Evidence includes: initial CPs do not undergo SAI, cannot occur inside another initial CP, and Dutch shows a special pronoun dat - all indicating a different position from ordinary DP subjects.

questionZh: Ross (1967) 的 Internal S Condition(禁止 CP 內嵌於另一 CP)能正確排除所有相關的不合語法句,因此 Adger 最終採用它。
questionEn: Ross's (1967) Internal S Condition (prohibiting CPs internal to other CPs) correctly rules out all relevant ungrammatical sentences, so Adger ultimately adopts it.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 錯誤。Internal S Condition 會誤排合語法的句子,如 "Believing [that the world is flat] gives one some solace",因此 Adger 改採 specifier-of-CP 分析。
explanationEn: False. The Internal S Condition wrongly rules out grammatical sentences like "Believing [that the world is flat] gives one some solace", so Adger instead adopts the specifier-of-CP analysis.

questionZh: Adger 用來發展非限定補語分析的兩條 θ-理論概括是什麼?
questionEn: What are the two θ-theory generalizations Adger uses to develop the analysis of infinitival complementation?

options.zh: 一個 DP 只能接受一個 θ-角色;且一個 DP 必須接受一個 θ-角色
options.en: A DP may receive only one θ-role; and a DP must receive a θ-role

options.zh: 一個 DP 必須接受 nominative;且一個 DP 只能在賓語位置
options.en: A DP must receive nominative; and a DP may only be in object position

options.zh: 每個子句必須有 C;且每個 C 必須帶 [Q]
options.en: Every clause must have a C; and every C must bear [Q]

options.zh: PRO 永遠被控制;且 PRO 永遠帶 accusative
options.en: PRO is always controlled; and PRO always bears accusative

explanationZh: (73): (a) 一個 DP 只能接受一個 θ-角色;(b) 一個 DP 必須接受一個 θ-角色。這兩條(θ-criterion)支撐了控制與提升的分析。
explanationEn: (73): (a) A DP may receive only one θ-role; (b) a DP must receive a θ-role. These two (the θ-criterion) underpin the control and raising analyses.

questionZh: 在「Medea tried [to PRO poison her children]」中,空主語 PRO 的指稱由誰決定?
questionEn: In "Medea tried [to PRO poison her children]", the reference of the null subject PRO is determined by what?

options.zh: 由主句主語 Medea 控制(controlled)
options.en: Controlled by the matrix subject Medea

options.zh: 由主句賓語控制
options.en: Controlled by the matrix object

options.zh: 由 C 上的 [Q] 特徵決定
options.en: Determined by the [Q] feature on C

options.zh: 永遠是任意指稱(arbitrary)
options.en: Always arbitrary in reference

explanationZh: PRO 在此被主句主語 Medea 控制(subject control),含 PRO 的子句稱為控制子句(Control Clauses)。PRO 也可有任意解讀,但此例為受控解讀。
explanationEn: Here PRO is controlled by the matrix subject Medea (subject control); clauses with PRO are Control Clauses. PRO can also have an arbitrary reading, but this example is a controlled one.

questionZh: Adger 提出控制子句中的 PRO 由一個帶 ________ 格特徵的空 C 賦值,其形態效果是使代詞不被發音。
questionEn: Adger proposes that PRO in control clauses is licensed by a null C bearing the ________ case feature, whose morphological effect is to render the pronoun unpronounced.
answerTextZh: null(空格,[null] case)
answerTextEn: null ([null] case)
explanationZh: 除了 [nom]、[acc]、[gen] 之外,Adger 新增 [null] 格特徵。控制動詞(try 類)選擇帶 [null] 的空 C,把代詞賦值為 [null],使其不被發音,即 PRO。
explanationEn: Besides [nom], [acc], [gen], Adger adds the [null] case feature. Control verbs (the try class) select a null C bearing [null], valuing the pronoun as [null] so it is unpronounced - i.e. PRO.

questionZh: 在 ECM(例外格標記)結構「We believed [him to be omnipotent]」中,內嵌子句主語 him 帶的格是 ________ 格,由上方 little v 的 [acc] 核查。
questionEn: In the ECM (Exceptional Case Marking) structure "We believed [him to be omnipotent]", the embedded subject him bears ________ case, checked by the [acc] of the higher little v.
answerTextZh: accusative(賓格)
answerTextEn: accusative
explanationZh: ECM 子句主語為 accusative(him 而非 he)。Adger 分析 ECM 動詞選一個非限定 TP,其主語的 accusative 由上方 little v 的 [acc] 核查。
explanationEn: The ECM subject is accusative (him, not he). Adger analyses ECM verbs as selecting a non-finite TP whose subject's accusative is checked by the [acc] of the higher little v.

questionZh: Adger 主張 ECM 子句是 TP(而非 CP),其主要論證之一來自假分裂句。下列哪項描述正確?
questionEn: Adger argues ECM clauses are TPs (not CPs); one main argument comes from pseudoclefting. Which description is correct?

options.zh: 控制子句與 for 子句可被假分裂,但 ECM 子句不可,因為 TP 不能被假分裂
options.en: Control clauses and for-clauses can be pseudoclefted, but ECM clauses cannot, because a TP cannot be pseudoclefted

options.zh: ECM 子句可被假分裂,但控制子句不可
options.en: ECM clauses can be pseudoclefted, but control clauses cannot

options.zh: 所有非限定子句都不能被假分裂
options.en: No non-finite clause can be pseudoclefted

options.zh: 只有帶 PRO 的子句才能被假分裂
options.en: Only clauses with PRO can be pseudoclefted

explanationZh: 控制子句與 for 子句(CP)可被假分裂,*What Medea believed was Jason to be a murderer 則不行。因為 ECM 子句是 TP,而 TP 不能被假分裂(*What I said that was we would go)。
explanationEn: Control and for-clauses (CPs) can be pseudoclefted, but *What Medea believed was Jason to be a murderer cannot. This follows if ECM clauses are TPs and a TP cannot be pseudoclefted (*What I said that was we would go).

questionZh: 在「I intended [for Jenny to be present]」中,Adger 把 for 分析為什麼?
questionEn: In "I intended [for Jenny to be present]", how does Adger analyse for?

options.zh: 非限定子句的標句詞 C,帶 [acc] 格特徵,核查內嵌主語的格
options.en: A complementizer C of the non-finite clause, bearing [acc] case, checking the embedded subject's case

options.zh: 介系詞 P,與 Jenny 構成 PP
options.en: A preposition P forming a PP with Jenny

options.zh: 帶 [null] 格的 C,要求 PRO
options.en: A C bearing [null] case, requiring PRO

options.zh: T 的一部分
options.en: Part of T

explanationZh: for 出現在子句主語之前並與 CP 成分,平行於限定子句中的 that,故為標句詞。它帶 [acc],把非限定子句主語賦值為 accusative,並排除 PRO(*for PRO to...)。
explanationEn: for occurs before the clausal subject and forms a constituent with CP, parallel to that in finite clauses, so it is a complementizer. It bears [acc], valuing the non-finite subject as accusative and ruling out PRO (*for PRO to...).

questionZh: 依 Adger 的分析,非限定 T(如 to)與限定 T 的差別在於:非限定 T 沒有格特徵,但仍保有 EPP 特徵。
questionEn: In Adger's analysis, non-finite T (e.g. to) differs from finite T in that it lacks a case feature but still bears an EPP feature.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 正確。非限定 T 沒有格特徵,故主語的格由別的成分(C 或上方投射)決定;但 EPP 特徵仍存在,因此驅動主語移到 spec-TP(決定詞序)。
explanationEn: True. Non-finite T lacks a case feature, so the subject's case comes from elsewhere (C or a higher projection); but the EPP feature remains, driving the subject to spec-TP (determining word order).

questionZh: 在提升(Raising)結構「Agamemnon seems to be a maniac」中,為何不能把它分析為 PRO(*Agamemnon seems [PRO to be a maniac])?
questionEn: In the raising structure "Agamemnon seems to be a maniac", why can it not be analysed with PRO (*Agamemnon seems [PRO to be a maniac])?

options.zh: seem 是單論元動詞,只有一個 θ-角色指派給子句;若用 PRO,主句主語 Agamemnon 將得不到 θ-角色,違反 θ-criterion
options.en: seem is a single-argument verb with only one θ-role assigned to the clause; with PRO the matrix subject Agamemnon would get no θ-role, violating the θ-criterion

options.zh: seem 不能選非限定子句
options.en: seem cannot select a non-finite clause

options.zh: PRO 不能出現在任何非限定子句
options.en: PRO cannot appear in any non-finite clause

options.zh: seem 指派 accusative 給 Agamemnon
options.en: seem assigns accusative to Agamemnon

explanationZh: seem/appear 是單論元動詞,其唯一 θ-角色指派給命題(子句)。若主語位置含 PRO,則主句主語 Agamemnon 將無 θ-角色,且不能是虛詞解讀,違反 θ-criterion。正確分析是 Agamemnon 從內嵌子句提升而來。
explanationEn: seem/appear are single-argument verbs whose only θ-role goes to the proposition (clause). With PRO in the subject position, the matrix subject Agamemnon would lack a θ-role and cannot be an expletive, violating the θ-criterion. The correct analysis is that Agamemnon raises from the embedded clause.

questionZh: 「Agamemnon seems to be a maniac」這類由 seem、appear 構成、主語經兩次 EPP 驅動移位的結構,其經典名稱為 ________。
questionEn: The construction formed by seem, appear (e.g. "Agamemnon seems to be a maniac"), where the subject undergoes two EPP-driven movements, has the classical name ________.
answerTextZh: 主語-到-主語提升(Subject-to-Subject Raising,或簡稱 Raising)
answerTextEn: Subject-to-Subject Raising (or simply Raising)
explanationZh: 主語先從 spec-vP 移到非限定 T 的指示語(由其 EPP 驅動),再移到主句 T 的指示語(由主句 T 的 EPP 驅動),經典名稱為 Subject-to-Subject Raising。
explanationEn: The subject moves first from spec-vP to the specifier of non-finite T (driven by its EPP), then to the specifier of matrix T (driven by matrix T's EPP); the classical name is Subject-to-Subject Raising.

questionZh: Adger 用什麼機制來阻止主語從「限定 TP」提升(*Agamemnon seems [(Agamemnon) has left])?
questionEn: What mechanism does Adger use to block a subject raising out of a finite TP (*Agamemnon seems [(Agamemnon) has left])?

options.zh: 限定 T 帶 [uclause-type: ] 特徵,若無 C 賦值便無法被核查賦值,加上 Extension Condition,使衍生 crash
options.en: Finite T bears an unvalued [uclause-type: ] feature; without a C to value it, and given the Extension Condition, the derivation crashes

options.zh: 限定 T 沒有 EPP 特徵
options.en: Finite T has no EPP feature

options.zh: seem 必須指派 accusative
options.en: seem must assign accusative

options.zh: 限定 T 帶 [null] 格
options.en: Finite T bears [null] case

explanationZh: 限定 T 帶不可解讀 [uclause-type: ];若 seem 選限定 TP,該特徵無自己的 C 可賦值,且依 Locality 與 Extension Condition,上方 T 或 C 也無法賦值它,故衍生 crash。結論:子句補語必須是 CP 或非限定 TP。
explanationEn: Finite T bears uninterpretable [uclause-type: ]; if seem selects a finite TP this feature has no C of its own to value it, and by Locality and the Extension Condition the higher T or C cannot value it either, so the derivation crashes. Conclusion: clausal complements must be CPs or non-finite TPs.

questionZh: Adger 用哪個證據顯示 persuade 類動詞(賓語控制)結構不同於 ECM 動詞結構?
questionEn: Which evidence does Adger use to show that persuade-class (object control) verbs differ structurally from ECM verbs?

options.zh: 虛詞分布(*I persuaded there to be a problem 不合法,但 I expected there to be a problem 合法)與被動釋義測試
options.en: Expletive distribution (*I persuaded there to be a problem is bad, but I expected there to be a problem is fine) and the passive-paraphrase test

options.zh: persuade 不能選子句補語
options.en: persuade cannot select a clausal complement

options.zh: persuade 子句主語永遠是 nominative
options.en: the subject of a persuade clause is always nominative

options.zh: ECM 動詞永遠帶 [Q] 特徵
options.en: ECM verbs always bear a [Q] feature

explanationZh: 兩個論證:(1) 虛詞 there 只能當主語,persuade 後的 DP 是賓語故拒絕 there,ECM 後的 NP 是內嵌主語故可;(2) 被動釋義:expect 句被動後釋義不變,persuade 句被動後釋義改變(說服對象改變),顯示 persuade 是三論元賓語控制(PRO),ECM 後 NP 是子句主語。
explanationEn: Two arguments: (1) expletive there can only be a subject; the DP after persuade is an object so rejects there, while the NP after an ECM verb is an embedded subject so allows it; (2) passive paraphrase: passivizing an expect complement preserves meaning, but passivizing a persuade complement changes who is persuaded - showing persuade is a three-place object-control verb (PRO), while the ECM NP is the clausal subject.

questionZh: Adger 採用哪一條經濟原則來排除 *There seem three men to be in the garden?請說明它如何運作。
questionEn: Which economy principle does Adger adopt to rule out *There seem three men to be in the garden, and how does it work?
answerTextZh: 「Merge 優先於 Move」(Merge is preferred over Move)。在滿足下層非限定 T 的 EPP 時,可選擇合併虛詞 there 或移動 three men;若 Merge 優先,只能合併 there,因此排除 *There seem three men...,得到正確的 There seemed to be three men in the garden。
answerTextEn: "Merge is preferred over Move." When satisfying the EPP of the lower non-finite T, one may either Merge the expletive there or Move three men; if Merge takes priority, only Merge is allowed, ruling out *There seem three men... and deriving the correct There seemed to be three men in the garden.
explanationZh: 正確。在滿足下層非限定 T 的 EPP 時,可選擇合併虛詞 there 或移動 three men;若 Merge 優先於 Move,只能合併 there,因此排除 *There seem three men to be in the garden,得到正確的 There seemed to be three men in the garden。
explanationEn: True. When satisfying the EPP of the lower non-finite T, one may either Merge the expletive there or Move three men; if Merge is preferred over Move, only Merge is allowed, ruling out *There seem three men to be in the garden and deriving the correct There seemed to be three men in the garden.

questionZh: 依 Adger 的分析,時間附加語子句(如 before/when/after 引介者)透過什麼方式整合進句樹,並附加到哪個投射?
questionEn: According to Adger, how are temporal adjunct clauses (introduced by before/when/after) integrated into the tree, and to which projection do they attach?

options.zh: 透過 Adjoin(非由特徵核查驅動,因此選擇性),附加到 TP
options.en: Via Adjoin (not feature-driven, hence optional), attaching to TP

options.zh: 透過 Merge,附加到 vP
options.en: Via Merge, attaching to vP

options.zh: 透過 Move,進入 spec-CP
options.en: Via Move, into spec-CP

options.zh: 透過格核查,附加到 DP
options.en: Via case checking, attaching to DP

explanationZh: 附加語 CP 由 Adjoin 整合(非特徵驅動,故選擇性)。時間附加語子句可附加在主句任一側,且出現在內嵌標句詞與主語之間,顯示它們附加到 TP。
explanationEn: Adjunct CPs are integrated by Adjoin (not feature-driven, hence optional). Temporal adjunct clauses can attach on either side of the main clause and appear between an embedding complementizer and the subject, indicating they adjoin to TP.

questionZh: Adger 把附加語 CP 分為哪兩大類?各舉一個引介它們的標句詞為例。
questionEn: Into which two main classes does Adger divide adjunct CPs? Give one example complementizer introducing each.
answerTextZh: (1) 時間關係類(temporal):before、when、after;(2) 原因/理由類(cause/reason):because、since、so that。
answerTextEn: (1) Temporal (time-relating): before, when, after; (2) cause/reason: because, since, so that.
explanationZh: Adger 將附加語 CP 分為兩類:以時間連結兩命題者(before/when/after),以及以原因或理由連結者(because/since/so that)。兩類在句法上都附加到 TP。
explanationEn: Adger divides adjunct CPs into those linking propositions by time (before/when/after) and those linking by cause or reason (because/since/so that). Both classes adjoin to TP.

questionZh: Adger 對德語 V2 的分析中,德語主句 C 把 T 上的 [uclause-type] 賦值為「強 ________」(使 T 移到 C),並另帶一個強的 [u________*] 特徵,吸引主題成分到 spec-CP。(請依序填入兩個空格)
questionEn: In Adger's analysis of German V2, matrix C values [uclause-type] on T as strong ________ (raising T to C), and additionally bears a strong [u________*] feature attracting a topic to spec-CP. (Fill the two blanks in order.)
answerTextZh: Decl;top(即強 Decl 值 + 強 [utop*] 主題特徵)
answerTextEn: Decl; top (strong Decl value + strong [utop*] topic feature)
explanationZh: 與英語不同,德語主句 C 對 [uclause-type] 的賦值(包括 Decl)永遠是強的,使 T(含已升入 T 的動詞)移到 C;C 還帶強 [utop*],吸引一個主題成分到 spec-CP,於是動詞落在第二位。
explanationEn: Unlike English, German matrix C always values [uclause-type] (including Decl) as strong, raising T (with the verb already in T) to C; C additionally bears strong [utop*], attracting a topic to spec-CP, so the verb lands in second position.

questionZh: 在德語中,由一般內嵌標句詞(如 dass)引導的從屬子句仍然觸發 V2,使動詞出現在第二位。
questionEn: In German, subordinate clauses headed by an ordinary embedding complementizer (e.g. dass) still trigger V2, putting the verb in second position.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 錯誤。一般內嵌 C(如 dass)不觸發 V2:在「Er sagte dass ich ... diesen Roman las」中動詞 las 出現在賓語之後,顯示德語底層是 OV 語言,只有主句 C 觸發 T-to-C(V2)。
explanationEn: False. An ordinary embedding C (e.g. dass) does not trigger V2: in "Er sagte dass ich ... diesen Roman las" the verb las appears after the object, showing German is underlyingly OV; only matrix C triggers T-to-C (V2).

questionZh: 在 "Who did Medea poison?" 這類 wh-問句中,wh-詞 who 最終落腳在哪個結構位置?
questionEn: In a wh-question like "Who did Medea poison?", which structural position does the wh-word who end up in?

options.zh: TP 的指示語 (Spec-TP)
options.en: the specifier of TP (Spec-TP)

options.zh: CP 的指示語 (Spec-CP)
options.en: the specifier of CP (Spec-CP)

options.zh: C 這個中心語位置
options.en: the head position C

options.zh: VP 補語的原位置
options.en: its base complement-of-V position

explanationZh: 比照 TP、vP、DP 的結構,Adger 假設移位的 wh-詞落在 CP 的指示語位置,提供了 C 有指示語的證據。who 原本與 poison 合併取得 Theme θ-角色,再移到 Spec-CP。
explanationEn: On analogy with TP, vP and DP, Adger hypothesizes that the moved wh-word lands in the specifier of CP, giving evidence that C projects a specifier. who is first Merged with poison for its Theme θ-role, then moves to Spec-CP.

questionZh: wh-移位是由 wh-詞開頭字母 (語音/拼字) 的特性所觸發的。
questionEn: Wh-movement is triggered by the phonological/spelling property of wh-words beginning with the letters wh.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: wh-移位不是由語音特性觸發。Adger 指出 *Whales have I seen、*Whisky do I drink 都不合語法;移位和其他移位一樣是由形態句法特徵 [wh] 觸發。how 不以 wh 開頭卻仍屬此類。
explanationEn: Wh-movement is not triggered by phonological features: *Whales have I seen and *Whisky do I drink are ungrammatical. Like all movement, it is triggered by the morphosyntactic feature [wh]. Note how does not begin with wh yet belongs to the class.

questionZh: 依 Adger 的分析,which 與 what 之所以能與名詞性補語結合形成較大的 wh-DP (如 [which poem about Achilles]),是因為它們的句法類別是什麼?
questionEn: According to Adger, which and what can combine with a nominal complement to form a larger wh-DP (e.g. [which poem about Achilles]) because their syntactic category is what?

options.zh: wh-限定詞 (wh-determiners),與 nP 補語結合
options.en: wh-determiners that combine with an nP complement

options.zh: wh-代詞 (pronominal),不投射更大結構
options.en: pronominal wh-forms that project no further

options.zh: wh-程度詞 (Deg),與 AP/AdvP 結合
options.en: wh-degree heads (Deg) combining with AP/AdvP

options.zh: wh-介詞 (P),與 DP 補語結合
options.en: wh-prepositions taking a DP complement

explanationZh: which 與 what 是限定詞的 [wh] 對應物,與 nP 補語結合;它們與一般 D 互補分布 (*Which the poem...)。相對地 who、what、when、where 是代詞性的,不能再帶補語。
explanationEn: which and what are the [wh] counterparts of determiners, combining with nP complements; they are in complementary distribution with ordinary Ds (*Which the poem...). By contrast who, what, when, where are pronominal and cannot take a further complement.

questionZh: Adger 把 how (如 [How fond of Clytemnestra]、[How quickly]) 分析為功能中心語 ______ 的 [wh] 版本,該中心語出現於 AP 與 AdvP 之上。
questionEn: Adger analyses how (as in [How fond of Clytemnestra], [How quickly]) as the [wh] version of the functional head ______, which appears above APs and AdvPs.
answerTextZh: Deg(ree) (程度詞)
answerTextEn: Deg(ree)
explanationZh: how 是程度詞 Deg (如 very) 的 [wh] 版本。這也促使 Adger 新增形容詞投射層級 (Adjectival: (Deg) > A),補進投射層級表 (Hierarchy of Projections)。
explanationEn: how is the [wh] version of the degree head Deg (like very). This also prompts Adger to add an Adjectival line ((Deg) > A) to the Hierarchy of Projections.

questionZh: 關於 [wh] 特徵的性質,下列哪一項符合 Adger 的主張?
questionEn: Regarding the nature of the [wh] feature, which statement matches Adger's view?

options.zh: 它是一元 (privative/atomic) 特徵,不參與 Agree 下的賦值,且跨語言形態規律支持它由 UG 提供
options.en: it is a privative (atomic) feature that does not participate in valuing under Agree, and cross-linguistic morphological regularities support it as a UG feature

options.zh: 它是二元 (binary) 特徵,值為 [+wh] 或 [-wh]
options.en: it is a binary feature valued [+wh] or [-wh]

options.zh: 它是語音特徵,由拼字 wh 決定
options.en: it is a phonological feature determined by the spelling wh

options.zh: 它只存在於印歐語系,非普遍特徵
options.en: it exists only in Indo-European and is not universal

explanationZh: [wh] 被當作一元特徵,不參與賦值。Zuni 的 wh-詞都以 -p 結尾、Kiowa 都以 h 開頭、Hebrew 多以 m 開頭,這些不相關語言的形態規律顯示 [wh] 有形態動機,屬於 UG 普遍可用的特徵 (土耳其語則無此規律)。
explanationEn: [wh] is treated as a privative feature that does not enter valuing. Zuni wh-words all end in -p, Kiowa ones begin with h, most Hebrew ones begin with m; such regularities across unrelated languages show [wh] is morphologically motivated as a UG feature (Turkish, however, lacks such regularity).

questionZh: Adger 用什麼語意手段來統一地釋義 (paraphrase) wh-問句?以 "Who ate the cake?" 為例說明。
questionEn: What semantic device does Adger use to uniformly paraphrase wh-questions? Illustrate with "Who ate the cake?".
answerTextZh: wh-問句可釋義為含有一個「疑問量詞 (interrogative quantifier)」的句子,形式為「For which x...」。例如 "Who ate the cake?" 釋義為「For which x, x a human, is it the case that x ate the cake?」。
answerTextEn: Wh-questions can be paraphrased as containing an interrogative quantifier of the form "For which x...". E.g. "Who ate the cake?" paraphrases as "For which x, x a human, is it the case that x ate the cake?".
explanationZh: Adger 指出 wh-詞語意相當一致,都可用疑問量詞「For which x...」來釋義 (when→x a time、why→x a reason、where→x a place 等)。形態 + 語意的共通性正是設立 [wh] 特徵的最強動機。
explanationEn: Adger notes wh-words share a uniform interpretation, all paraphrasable with the interrogative quantifier "For which x..." (when→x a time, why→x a reason, where→x a place, etc.). The shared morphology plus semantics is the strongest motivation for the [wh] feature.

questionZh: 在 Adger 的系統中,觸發 wh-詞移到 Spec-CP 的是哪一個特徵?
questionEn: In Adger's system, which feature triggers movement of a wh-phrase to Spec-CP?

options.zh: wh-詞本身可解讀的 [wh] 特徵
options.en: the interpretable [wh] feature on the wh-phrase itself

options.zh: C[Q] 上一個強的、不可解讀的 [uwh*] 特徵
options.en: a strong uninterpretable [uwh*] feature on C[Q]

options.zh: T 上的 EPP 特徵
options.en: the EPP feature on T

options.zh: T 上的強 [Q*] 特徵
options.en: the strong [Q*] feature on T

explanationZh: C[Q] 可帶一個強的 [uwh*] 特徵,它與其 c-command 的 wh-詞核查 (Agree)。因為 [uwh*] 是強的,要求表面局域性,wh-詞 (為詞組) 必須移入 Spec-CP。wh-詞自身的 [wh] 是可解讀的,不需被核查。
explanationEn: C[Q] may bear a strong [uwh*] feature that checks (Agree) with a wh-phrase it c-commands. Because [uwh*] is strong, it forces surface locality, so the wh-phrase (being phrasal) must move to Spec-CP. The wh-phrase's own [wh] is interpretable and needs no checking.

questionZh: [uwh*] 之強度所強制的局域性,在技術上是藉由把該特徵「投射 (project)」到 ______ 這個層級來達成,正如 EPP 投射到 T、[gen*] 投射到 D 一樣。
questionEn: The locality enforced by the strength of [uwh*] is technically achieved by projecting that feature to the ______ level, just as the EPP feature is projected to T and [gen*] to D.
answerTextZh: C (the C level / C 層級)
answerTextEn: the C level (C)
explanationZh: 強特徵的局域性是把該特徵投射到 C 層級來達成。當 wh-詞與 C 合併 (Merge) 時,它就與這個強 wh-特徵處於姊妹關係 (sisterhood),滿足表面局域性。
explanationEn: Locality of the strong feature is achieved by projecting it to the C level. When the wh-phrase Merges with C, it is in a sisterhood relation with the strong wh-feature, satisfying surface locality.

questionZh: Adger 為何拒絕「在 wh-詞上設一個強 [uQ*] 特徵以強迫其移位」這個提案?
questionEn: Why does Adger reject the proposal that the wh-word itself bears a strong [uQ*] feature forcing it to move?

options.zh: 因為 wh-詞從不移動到 Spec-CP
options.en: because wh-words never move to Spec-CP

options.zh: 因為迴聲問句與多重 wh-問句中,wh-詞可合法地停在原位 (in situ)
options.en: because in echo-questions and multiple wh-questions wh-words can grammatically stay in situ

options.zh: 因為 [uQ*] 與 EPP 衝突
options.en: because [uQ*] conflicts with the EPP

options.zh: 因為英語沒有 yes/no 問句
options.en: because English has no yes/no questions

explanationZh: 若 wh-詞自帶強 [uQ*] 必移,則無法解釋它能合法停在原位:迴聲問句 (Medea saw who?) 及多重 wh-問句 (Who poisoned who?) 中至少一個 wh-詞在原位。因此移位動機應在 C 上的 [uwh*],而非 wh-詞本身。
explanationEn: If wh-words carried a strong [uQ*] forcing movement, we couldn't explain their grammatical in-situ uses: echo-questions (Medea saw who?) and multiple wh-questions (Who poisoned who?) leave at least one wh-word in situ. So the trigger must be [uwh*] on C, not a feature on the wh-word.

questionZh: 迴聲問句 (echo-questions) 如 "Medea saw who?" 與一般 wh-問句一樣,都帶有 C 上的 [Q] 特徵與「for which x」的疑問量詞語意。
questionEn: Echo-questions like "Medea saw who?" carry the [Q] feature on C and the "for which x" interrogative-quantifier semantics, just like ordinary wh-questions.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 迴聲問句不是一般意義的疑問句。其釋義不是「for which x...」,而是「重複 who 在句中所代表的詞」,通常表驚訝或要求澄清。Adger 結論:若迴聲問句中有 C,它不帶 [Q]。
explanationEn: Echo-questions are not questions in the usual sense. Their paraphrase is not "for which x..." but "repeat the word who stands for", typically expressing surprise or a request for clarity. Adger concludes that if there is a C in an echo-question, it does not bear [Q].

questionZh: 若把 C[Q] 上的 [uwh*] 訂為「強制 (obligatory)」,Adger 如何解釋沒有外顯 wh-詞的 yes/no 問句 (如 "Did Medea poison Jason?")?
questionEn: If [uwh*] on C[Q] is made obligatory, how does Adger account for yes/no questions with no overt wh-word (e.g. "Did Medea poison Jason?")?

options.zh: 由一個直接 Merge 在 Spec-CP 的零運算子 (null operator) Op[wh] 來滿足 [uwh*]
options.en: a null operator Op[wh] Merged directly in Spec-CP satisfies [uwh*]

options.zh: 助動詞 did 本身帶 [wh] 特徵滿足 [uwh*]
options.en: the auxiliary did itself bears [wh] and satisfies [uwh*]

options.zh: 主語移到 Spec-CP 滿足 [uwh*]
options.en: the subject moves to Spec-CP to satisfy [uwh*]

options.zh: yes/no 問句的 C 不帶 [Q],故無需 [uwh*]
options.en: the C of a yes/no question lacks [Q], so [uwh*] is not needed

explanationZh: Adger 假設強制的 [uwh*] 由一個帶 [wh] 的零運算子 (Op) 直接 Merge 在 Spec-CP 來滿足。此 Op 的語意不是疑問量詞,而是「命題 p 是真是假?」,正好給出 yes/no 問句的語意。
explanationEn: Adger assumes the obligatory [uwh*] is satisfied by a null operator (Op) bearing [wh] Merged directly in Spec-CP. Its semantics is not an interrogative quantifier but "is it true or false that p?", yielding yes/no question meaning.

questionZh: 在 "*Did Medea poison who?" 的推導中,當 T 已升到 C[Q, uwh*] 且詞彙序列 (numeration) 含一個零運算子時,Adger 用哪個原則迫使我們不去移動 who?
questionEn: In deriving "*Did Medea poison who?", when T has raised to C[Q, uwh*] and the numeration contains a null operator, which principle forces us not to move who?

options.zh: 優位條件 (Superiority)
options.en: the Superiority condition

options.zh: Merge 優於 Move (preference for Merge over Move)
options.en: the preference for Merge over Move

options.zh: 最後手段 (Last Resort)
options.en: Last Resort

options.zh: 投射層級 (Hierarchy of Projections)
options.en: the Hierarchy of Projections

explanationZh: 面對「升 who」或「Merge 零運算子」兩種選項,Merge 優於 Move 強迫我們 Merge 零運算子。結果結構含兩個 wh-成分 (Op 與 who),語意上無法把一個 yes/no 運算子與一個疑問量詞組合,導致語意不連貫而被排除——句法良構卻語意亂碼。
explanationEn: Faced with a choice of raising who or Merging the null operator, Merge-over-Move forces us to Merge the operator. The result has two wh-elements (Op and who); semantically a yes/no operator cannot combine with an interrogative quantifier, so it is ruled out as well-formed syntax but semantic gibberish.

questionZh: wh-移位也能作用於介詞賓語與附加語 (如 "Who did you introduce Athena to?"、"Where did Perseus see the Gorgon?")。請說明這些 wh-成分在推導中的起點與終點。
questionEn: Wh-movement also applies to objects of prepositions and adjuncts (e.g. "Who did you introduce Athena to?", "Where did Perseus see the Gorgon?"). State where these wh-elements start and end in the derivation.
answerTextZh: 每個 wh-成分都先在其底層位置 Merge:介詞賓語先與介詞 Merge (PP 再與動詞投射 Merge);附加語 (時間/方式/原因) 則附加於 vP。之後該 wh-成分移到 Spec-CP。
answerTextEn: Each wh-element is first Merged in its base position: a P-object Merges with the preposition (the PP then Merging with a verbal projection); an adjunct (temporal/manner/causal) adjoins to vP. The wh-element then moves to Spec-CP.
explanationZh: Adger 處理 wh-賓語、介詞賓語與附加語的方式相同:都在原位 Merge 取得詮釋/θ-關係,再因 C 上的 [uwh*] 移到 Spec-CP。介詞賓語留下介詞 (to (who)),附加語從 vP 附加位置移出。
explanationEn: Adger treats wh-objects, P-objects and adjuncts alike: each Merges in base position for interpretation/θ-relations, then moves to Spec-CP because of [uwh*] on C. The P-object strands its preposition (to (who)); the adjunct moves from its vP-adjoined site.

questionZh: 在標準英語的嵌入式 wh-問句 (如 "I wondered who Medea had poisoned") 中,會發生 T 到 C 的移位 (倒裝)。
questionEn: In Standard English embedded wh-questions (e.g. "I wondered who Medea had poisoned"), T-to-C movement (inversion) takes place.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 標準英語嵌入式 wh-問句不發生 T-to-C 移位 (*I wondered who had Medea poisoned)。Adger 把這歸因於嵌入式 C 的詞彙特性:它把 T 的 [uclause-type] 賦值為弱 [Q] 而非強 [Q*],故 T 不上升。但 wh-詞仍移到嵌入 CP 的指示語。
explanationEn: Standard English embedded wh-questions show no T-to-C movement (*I wondered who had Medea poisoned). Adger attributes this to the embedded C valuing [uclause-type] on T as weak [Q] rather than strong [Q*], so T does not raise. The wh-word still moves to the embedded Spec-CP.

questionZh: 為什麼把主語 wh-問句 (如 "Who drank the poison?") 比照賓語 wh-問句處理會出問題?
questionEn: Why does treating subject wh-questions (e.g. "Who drank the poison?") just like object wh-questions create a problem?

options.zh: 因為主語 wh-詞無法取得 θ-角色
options.en: because the subject wh-word cannot receive a θ-role

options.zh: 因為它預測會發生 T-to-C 移位而觸發 do-support,但 "Who drank..." 並無 do
options.en: because it predicts T-to-C movement triggering do-support, yet "Who drank..." has no do

options.zh: 因為主語 wh-詞不帶 [wh] 特徵
options.en: because the subject wh-word lacks a [wh] feature

options.zh: 因為主語不能移到 Spec-CP
options.en: because subjects cannot move to Spec-CP

explanationZh: 賓語分析需要 T-to-C 移位;若主語問句也如此,當 T 只含 T 中心語而無助動詞時,T-to-C 會打斷 T–v 鏈而觸發 do-support,預測 *Who did drink the poison? 才合法 (但只在強調義成立)。實際上不帶 do 的 "Who drank the poison?" 才正常,故須特別處理。
explanationEn: The object analysis requires T-to-C movement; if subject questions worked the same way, with only the T head and no auxiliary, T-to-C would break the T–v chain and trigger do-support, predicting *Who did drink the poison? (only fine emphatically). In fact the do-less "Who drank the poison?" is the normal form, so a special treatment is needed.

questionZh: Adger 解決主語 wh-問句的關鍵假設是:由於主語 c-command T,可以讓「主語」而非 C 來核查 T 上的 [uclause-type];具體規定為:T 上的 [uclause-type] 可以有 ______ 作為其值。
questionEn: Adger's key assumption for subject wh-questions is that, since the subject c-commands T, the subject (rather than C) can check [uclause-type] on T; specifically: [uclause-type] on T may have ______ as a value.
answerTextZh: [wh]
answerTextEn: [wh]
explanationZh: 主語的關鍵句法特性是它 c-command T。Adger 規定 [uclause-type] 可取 [wh] 為值:wh-主語升到 Spec-TP 後與 T 上的 [uclause-type] Agree 並賦值為 [wh],此特徵即被核查,故 C 與 T 不再 Agree,不發生 T-to-C 移位,也就沒有 do-support。
explanationEn: The crucial property of subjects is that they c-command T. Adger stipulates that [uclause-type] may take [wh] as a value: once the wh-subject raises to Spec-TP it Agrees with [uclause-type] on T, valuing it [wh] and checking it, so C and T no longer Agree, no T-to-C movement occurs, and no do-support arises.

questionZh: 關於長距離 wh-問句 (如 "Who did Jason think that Medea had poisoned?"),Adger 最終支持哪一種推導?
questionEn: For long-distance wh-questions (e.g. "Who did Jason think that Medea had poisoned?"), which derivation does Adger ultimately support?

options.zh: wh-詞從原位一步直接移到主句 Spec-CP
options.en: the wh-word moves in one step from base to the matrix Spec-CP

options.zh: 連續循環移位:先移到嵌入 CP 的指示語,再移到主句 Spec-CP
options.en: successive-cyclic movement: first to the embedded Spec-CP, then to the matrix Spec-CP

options.zh: wh-詞留在原位,由 C 長距離 Agree
options.en: the wh-word stays in situ, with long-distance Agree by C

options.zh: wh-詞先移到 Spec-TP 再移到主句 Spec-CP
options.en: the wh-word moves to Spec-TP then to matrix Spec-CP

explanationZh: 雖然單步移位看似較簡省,但跨語言證據 (如 Scottish Gaelic 的中介標句詞變化) 顯示移位是連續循環的:wh-詞先停在嵌入 CP 的指示語,再移到主句 Spec-CP,不能跳過任何 Spec-CP。
explanationEn: Although one-step movement seems more economical, cross-linguistic evidence (e.g. intermediate complementizer alternation in Scottish Gaelic) shows movement is successive-cyclic: the wh-word stops in the embedded Spec-CP before moving to the matrix Spec-CP, and cannot skip a Spec-CP.

questionZh: 在 Scottish Gaelic 的長距離 wh-問句中,中介標句詞發生什麼變化,而這如何支持 Adger 的分析?
questionEn: In Scottish Gaelic long-distance wh-questions, what happens to the intermediate complementizer, and how does this support Adger's analysis?

options.zh: 由一般嵌入用的 gu(n) 變成 a 形式,顯示中介 C 帶強 [uwh*],wh-詞移經其指示語
options.en: it changes from ordinary embedding gu(n) to a, showing the intermediate C bears strong [uwh*] and the wh-word moves through its specifier

options.zh: 完全消失,顯示移位是單步的
options.en: it disappears entirely, showing movement is in one step

options.zh: 由 a 變成 gu(n),顯示 wh-詞留在原位
options.en: it changes from a to gu(n), showing the wh-word stays in situ

options.zh: 不變,顯示中介 CP 與移位無關
options.en: it stays unchanged, showing the intermediate CP is irrelevant to movement

explanationZh: Scottish Gaelic 一般嵌入標句詞為 gu(n);形成長距離 wh-問句時中介標句詞變為 a。Adger 解釋:中介 C 帶強 [uwh*],wh-詞移入其指示語核查,gu(n) 則無此特徵。這支持連續循環移位。
explanationEn: The ordinary embedding complementizer is gu(n); in long-distance wh-questions the intermediate complementizer becomes a. Adger says the intermediate C bears a strong [uwh*] checked by moving the wh-word into its specifier, while gu(n) lacks this feature, supporting successive-cyclic movement.

questionZh: Adger 從 Scottish Gaelic 的證據暫時接受一條規定 (stipulation):wh-詞移動時不能跳過 (skip) 任何 ______。
questionEn: From the Scottish Gaelic evidence, Adger provisionally adopts the stipulation that a wh-expression cannot skip a ______ when it moves.
answerTextZh: CP 的指示語 (specifier of CP / Spec-CP)
answerTextEn: specifier of CP (Spec-CP)
explanationZh: 規定為:「A wh-expression cannot skip a specifier of CP when it moves.」Gaelic 的證據顯示移位由 Spec-CP 到 Spec-CP 進行,但這條限制本身尚未被推導,Adger 留待下一章 (局域性) 處理。
explanationEn: The stipulation is: "A wh-expression cannot skip a specifier of CP when it moves." The Gaelic evidence shows movement proceeds Spec-CP to Spec-CP, but the restriction itself is not yet derived; Adger leaves it to the next chapter on locality.

questionZh: 在多重 wh-問句 "Who poisoned who?" 的推導中,為什麼只有一個 wh-詞移到 Spec-CP?
questionEn: In the derivation of the multiple wh-question "Who poisoned who?", why does only one wh-word move to Spec-CP?

options.zh: 因為賓語 who 沒有 [wh] 特徵
options.en: because the object who lacks a [wh] feature

options.zh: 因為 C 上的單一強 [uwh*] 一旦被主語 who 核查就滿足了,賓語 who 可留原位 (其 [wh] 可解讀)
options.en: because the single strong [uwh*] on C, once checked by the subject who, is satisfied, leaving the object who in situ (its [wh] is interpretable)

options.zh: 因為兩個 wh-詞都移到 Spec-CP 但其中一個被刪除
options.en: because both wh-words move to Spec-CP but one is deleted

options.zh: 因為英語不允許多重 wh-問句
options.en: because English disallows multiple wh-questions

explanationZh: wh-詞的 [wh] 是可解讀的,無需核查;移位只因 C 上的單一強 [uwh*]。主語 who 升到 Spec-CP 核查掉 [uwh*] 後,需求即滿足,賓語 who 留在原位。最終所有不可解讀特徵都已核查,語意得到「for which pair x and y」的雙重疑問解讀。
explanationEn: A wh-word's [wh] is interpretable and needs no checking; movement is forced only by the single strong [uwh*] on C. Once the subject who raises to Spec-CP and checks [uwh*], the requirement is met and the object who stays in situ. All uninterpretable features are checked, yielding the pair reading "for which pair x and y".

questionZh: Adger 如何排除 "*[CP Who [TP who poisoned]]" 這種賓語跨越主語移位的句子 (即優位效應 Superiority)?
questionEn: How does Adger rule out "*[CP Who [TP who poisoned]]", where the object moves across the subject (the Superiority effect)?

options.zh: 由 Merge 優於 Move 原則
options.en: by the Merge-over-Move principle

options.zh: 因為一個中心語只核查它 c-command 範圍內最近的匹配特徵,主語 wh-詞介於 C 與賓語之間
options.en: because a head checks the closest matching feature it c-commands, and the subject wh-word intervenes between C and the object

options.zh: 因為賓語不能取得格
options.en: because the object cannot get Case

options.zh: 因為這需要 do-support
options.en: because it would require do-support

explanationZh: 優位效應由既有的局域核查機制自然導出:中心語核查其 c-command 範圍內最近的匹配特徵。C 與 TP 合併時最近的 wh-詞是主語 (介於 C 與賓語之間),故升的是主語而非賓語。三論元動詞中 Theme 比 Goal 近,也預測 Theme 先移 (Who...introduce to whom)。
explanationEn: Superiority follows from the existing locality of checking: a head checks the closest matching feature it c-commands. When C merges with TP the closest wh-word is the subject (intervening between C and the object), so the subject raises, not the object. With three-argument verbs the Theme is closer than the Goal, predicting the Theme moves (Who...introduce to whom).

questionZh: 當把簡單 wh-詞換成含 which 的較複雜片語時,兩種 wh-詞序皆可成立 (如 "Which poet wrote which ode?" 與 "Which ode did which poet write?"),這稱為 D(iscourse)-linking 效應。
questionEn: When simple wh-words are replaced by more complex which-phrases, both wh-orders become well formed (e.g. "Which poet wrote which ode?" and "Which ode did which poet write?"); this is the D(iscourse)-linking effect.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 正確。優位是個弱現象:含 which 的 D-linked 片語可繞過優位,兩種詞序皆可。D-linking 指 wh-限定詞範限於語境中已確立的對象 (故進入暗房聽到聲音說 "Who's there?" 自然,而 #"Which person is there?" 不妥)。Adger 不在本書解釋此效應。
explanationEn: Correct. Superiority is weak: D-linked which-phrases can bypass it, both orders being acceptable. D-linking is when the wh-determiner ranges over discourse-established entities (so "Who's there?" is natural on entering a dark room, but #"Which person is there?" is odd). Adger does not explain this effect in the book.

questionZh: 依 Adger 的參數化分析,日語 (與中文) 之所以普遍呈現 wh-in situ (連簡單 wh-問句也不移位),是因為 C[Q] 上的 [uwh] 特徵具有什麼性質?
questionEn: On Adger's parametric analysis, Japanese (and Chinese) show generalized wh-in situ (even simple wh-questions don't move) because the [uwh] feature on C[Q] has which property?

options.zh: 它是強的 (strong)
options.en: it is strong

options.zh: 它是弱的 (weak),因此只透過 Agree 核查而不引發移位
options.en: it is weak, so it is checked via Agree without triggering movement

options.zh: 它完全不存在
options.en: it is entirely absent

options.zh: 它是可解讀的 (interpretable)
options.en: it is interpretable

explanationZh: 跨語言變異綁定在功能中心語特徵的強弱上。日語、中文 C[Q] 帶弱 [uwh]:它與 wh-賓語建立 Agree 關係而核查,但不引發移位,故 wh-詞留在一般 (如賓格) 位置。日語為中心語在後 (head-final),標句詞 ka 出現在屈折動詞右側。
explanationEn: Cross-linguistic variation is tied to the strength of features on functional heads. Japanese and Chinese have a weak [uwh] on C[Q]: it is checked via an Agree relation with the wh-object but triggers no movement, so the wh-word stays in its normal (e.g. accusative) position. Japanese is head-final, with the complementizer ka to the right of the inflected verb.

questionZh: 保加利亞語 (Bulgarian) 的多重 wh-前移 (如 "Kogo kakvo e pital Ivan") 為何對 Adger 目前的系統造成困擾?
questionEn: Why does Bulgarian multiple wh-fronting (e.g. "Kogo kakvo e pital Ivan") pose a problem for Adger's current system?
answerTextZh: 因為保加利亞語把所有 wh-片語都移到子句外圍位置。但依目前假設,一旦一個 wh-片語移到 Spec-CP 核查掉強 wh-特徵,推導就應收斂,其餘 wh-片語應留原位。所以需要額外機制才能解釋為何全部 wh-片語都前移。
answerTextEn: Because Bulgarian moves all wh-phrases to a clause-peripheral position. But on the current assumptions, once one wh-phrase moves to Spec-CP and checks the strong wh-feature, the derivation should converge with all other wh-phrases in situ. So something extra is needed to capture why every wh-phrase fronts.
explanationZh: Adger 的系統預測只需一個 wh-片語移位即可滿足 C 上的強 [uwh*],其餘留原位 (如英語多重問句)。保加利亞語卻把全部 wh-片語前移,出乎預期。Adger 不在此深入,僅指向延伸閱讀 (multiple wh-fronting 語言)。
explanationEn: Adger's system predicts that only one wh-phrase needs to move to satisfy the strong [uwh*] on C, leaving the rest in situ (as in English multiple questions). Bulgarian instead fronts all wh-phrases, which is unexpected. Adger does not pursue it, pointing to further reading on multiple wh-fronting languages.

questionZh: Adger 在本章總結指出:把 DP-移位系統延伸到 wh-移位大致成功,只需要一條額外規定來處理主語 wh-問句中缺少 T-to-C 移位的情形。
questionEn: In the chapter summary, Adger notes that extending the DP-movement system to wh-movement was largely successful, needing only a single stipulation to handle the lack of T-to-C movement in subject wh-questions.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 正確。本章未引入太多新理論機制,而是把處理 DP-移位的系統延伸到 wh-移位,大體成功,只需一條規定 (即 [uclause-type] 可取 [wh] 為值) 來處理主語 wh-問句不發生 T-to-C 移位的情形。
explanationEn: Correct. The chapter introduces few new theoretical mechanisms, instead extending the DP-movement system to wh-movement, largely successfully, with only a single stipulation (that [uclause-type] may take [wh] as a value) to handle the absence of T-to-C movement in subject wh-questions.

questionZh: 在練習中,Adger 假設 wh-關係子句與 wh-問句相同,差別在於領導它們的 C 帶有哪一個可解讀的子句類型特徵?
questionEn: In the exercises, Adger assumes wh-relative clauses are just like wh-questions except that the C heading them bears which interpretable clause-type feature?

options.zh: [Rel]
options.en: [Rel]

options.zh: [Decl]
options.en: [Decl]

options.zh: [Top]
options.en: [Top]

explanationZh: 關係子句被當作附加於 nP 層的 CP。其 C 帶可解讀的子句類型特徵 [Rel] (而非 [Q]),所以被詮釋為關係修飾語而非問句。wh-關係詞 (如 which、who、when、where) 經運算子移位到 Spec-CP。
explanationEn: Relative clauses are treated as CPs adjoined at the nP level. Their C bears the interpretable clause-type feature [Rel] (not [Q]), so they are interpreted as relative modifiers rather than questions. The wh-relative (which, who, when, where) undergoes operator movement to Spec-CP.

questionZh: wh-關係子句 (如 "the book which Anson lent to Jenny") 與 that-關係子句 (如 "the book that Anson lent to Jenny") 在分析上有何不同?把 wh-分析直接套用到 that-關係子句會遇到什麼問題?
questionEn: How do wh-relatives (e.g. "the book which Anson lent to Jenny") differ analytically from that-relatives (e.g. "the book that Anson lent to Jenny")? What problem arises in carrying the wh-analysis over to that-relatives?
answerTextZh: wh-關係子句有外顯的 wh-運算子移到 Spec-CP,可用 c-選擇論證來證明涉及移位 (留下空位)。that-關係子句只有標句詞 that、沒有外顯 wh-詞,無法直接以同樣的「外顯片語移位」論證;可行的解法是假設有一個帶 [wh] 的零運算子 (null operator) 移到 Spec-CP,平行於 yes/no 問句中的 Op。
answerTextEn: wh-relatives have an overt wh-operator moving to Spec-CP, and the c-selectional gap argument shows movement is involved. that-relatives have only the complementizer that and no overt wh-word, so the same overt-phrase-movement argument cannot apply directly; a way around it is to posit a null operator bearing [wh] that moves to Spec-CP, parallel to the Op in yes/no questions.
explanationZh: 練習 3 Part D 要求比較兩類關係子句。wh-關係子句靠外顯 wh-詞移位 (留下被選擇位置的空位) 來論證移位;that-關係子句缺外顯 wh-詞,問題在於無外顯成分可移。最常見的解法是引入零運算子 (Op[wh]) 移到 Spec-CP,與本章 yes/no 問句的 null operator 機制平行。
explanationEn: Exercise 3 Part D compares the two relative types. wh-relatives argue for movement via an overt wh-word leaving a selected-position gap; that-relatives lack an overt wh-word, so the problem is there is no overt element to move. The standard fix is to introduce a null operator (Op[wh]) moving to Spec-CP, parallel to the null operator used for yes/no questions in the chapter.

questionZh: 練習 4 探討否定移位 (Negative movement,如 "Never will I do syntax again")。Adger 要求學生把這類否定片語前移與平行的 ______ 移位例子做比較並推導。
questionEn: Exercise 4 explores Negative movement (e.g. "Never will I do syntax again"). Adger asks students to compare such negative fronting with parallel ______ movement examples and hypothesize a derivation.
answerTextZh: wh-移位 (wh-movement)
answerTextEn: wh-movement
explanationZh: 否定移位把否定片語移到句首並常伴隨倒裝 (Never will I...),與 wh-移位平行 (都是運算子移到 CP 域並可觸發 T-to-C)。練習要學生比照 wh-移位推導否定移位,並檢驗 "I swore that never again would I drink absinthe" 這類嵌入式否定倒裝是否符合或挑戰此分析。
explanationEn: Negative movement fronts a negative phrase, often with inversion (Never will I...), parallel to wh-movement (both are operator movements to the CP domain that can trigger T-to-C). The exercise asks students to derive negative movement on analogy with wh-movement and test whether embedded cases like "I swore that never again would I drink absinthe" fit or challenge the analysis.

questionZh: 依本章的核心主張,看似無界限的長距離 wh-移位實際上是如何進行的?
questionEn: According to the central claim of this chapter, how does apparently unbounded long-distance wh-movement actually proceed?

options.zh: 以一連串短的、局域的步驟進行(連續循環移位)
options.en: In a series of short, local steps (successive cyclic movement)

options.zh: 以單一一個長跳躍從起點直達終點
options.en: In a single long leap from launch site to landing site

options.zh: 完全不涉及移位,只是基底生成
options.en: It involves no movement at all, only base generation

options.zh: 只能在單一子句內進行
options.en: It can only take place within a single clause

explanationZh: 本章主張長距離 wh-移位雖可移到看似無界限的距離,但總是經由一連串短的局域步驟(連續循環移位),每一步都瞄準一個 CP 的指示語,而非單一長跳躍。
explanationEn: The chapter argues that long-distance wh-movement, though spanning apparently unbounded distances, always involves a number of short local steps (successive cyclic movement), each targeting a specifier of CP, rather than a single long leap.

questionZh: 「連續循環移位(successive cyclic movement)」這個術語指的是衍生過程依序往上循環經過各個 CP。
questionEn: The term 'successive cyclic movement' refers to a derivation that successively cycles up through CPs.

options.zh: 真
options.en: True

options.zh: 假
options.en: False

explanationZh: 文中明言:以這種方式進行的衍生稱為連續循環移位,因為它依序往上循環經過各個 CP。
explanationEn: The text states that a derivation proceeding this way involves successive cyclic movement, since it successively cycles up through CPs in the derivation.

questionZh: 在 West Ulster 英語中,可以附著到 wh-詞並被「漂浮(stranded)」的量詞是哪一個?
questionEn: In West Ulster English, which quantifier can attach to a wh-word and be 'stranded'?

options.zh: all
options.en: all

options.zh: each
options.en: each

options.zh: both
options.en: both

options.zh: some
options.en: some

explanationZh: 在 West Ulster 英語中,量詞 all 可附著到 wh-詞(如 What all、Who all),且可被漂浮留下,例如 "What did you get all for Xmas?"。
explanationEn: In West Ulster English the quantifier 'all' can attach to a wh-word (e.g. What all, Who all) and can be stranded, as in 'What did you get all for Xmas?'.

questionZh: 在 West Ulster 英語的 "What did he say [all (that) he wanted]?" 中,被漂浮的量詞 all 出現在中介子句邊界,這支持了什麼結論?
questionEn: In West Ulster English's 'What did he say [all (that) he wanted]?', the stranded quantifier 'all' appears at the intermediate clause boundary. What does this support?

options.zh: wh-移位先瞄準中介 CP 的指示語,提供量詞可被漂浮的位置
options.en: Wh-movement first targets the intermediate Spec-CP, providing a position where the quantifier can be stranded

options.zh: wh-移位一定是單步長跳躍
options.en: Wh-movement must be a single long leap

options.zh: 量詞 all 無法被漂浮
options.en: The quantifier 'all' cannot be stranded

options.zh: 中介 CP 不能含有指示語位置
options.en: Intermediate CPs cannot contain a specifier position

explanationZh: 若 wh-移位先瞄準中介 CP 指示語,就預期量詞可在該中介位置被漂浮留下,而資料正好證實這點;若移位是單步跳過該位置,則無從產生此結構。
explanationEn: If wh-movement first targets the intermediate Spec-CP, the quantifier should be strandable there; the data confirm this. A one-step derivation skipping that position could not generate such a structure.

questionZh: West Ulster 英語的量詞漂浮論證只能證明連續步驟移位「至少______」,並未證明單一長跳躍(one long swoop)是錯誤的。
questionEn: The West Ulster English quantifier-stranding argument only shows that successive-step movement must at least be ______; it does not prove the one-long-swoop derivation is wrong.
answerTextZh: 被允許(insufficient/不足夠,而非錯誤)
answerTextEn: allowed (the long swoop is insufficient, not wrong)
explanationZh: 假。量詞漂浮只證明連續步驟移位「至少必須被允許」,長跳躍方式並非錯誤、只是不足夠;真正排除長跳躍的是 Gaelic 標句詞一致與西班牙語倒裝的論證。
explanationEn: False. Quantifier stranding only shows successive-step movement must at least be allowed; the long-swoop approach is not wrong, just insufficient. It is the Gaelic complementizer-agreement and Spanish inversion arguments that rule out the long swoop.

questionZh: 在 Scottish Gaelic 中,當 wh-移位跨越一個嵌入子句時,嵌入標句詞會出現何種變化?
questionEn: In Scottish Gaelic, what happens to the embedded complementizer when wh-movement crosses an embedded clause?

options.zh: 原本預期的 gu(n) 不出現,改用標句詞 a
options.en: The expected gu(n) does not appear; the complementizer 'a' is used instead

options.zh: 兩個標句詞 gu(n) 與 a 都必須出現
options.en: Both gu(n) and 'a' must appear

options.zh: 標句詞完全消失,沒有任何標記
options.en: The complementizer disappears entirely with no marking

options.zh: 標句詞變成英語的 that
options.en: The complementizer becomes the English 'that'

explanationZh: 當成分自嵌入子句 wh-移位至母句 Spec-CP 時,原預期的 gu 不出現,改用標句詞 a(伴隨後續動詞形式變化),顯示 wh-語自嵌入 Spec-CP 經過。
explanationEn: When a constituent wh-moves out of an embedded clause into the matrix Spec-CP, the expected gu does not appear; instead the complementizer 'a' shows up (with a concomitant verb-form change), revealing passage through the embedded Spec-CP.

questionZh: 為什麼 Scottish Gaelic 的標句詞一致論證比 West Ulster 量詞漂浮論證更強?它額外排除了什麼?
questionEn: Why is the Scottish Gaelic complementizer-agreement argument stronger than the West Ulster quantifier-stranding argument? What does it additionally rule out?
answerTextZh: 因為當 wh-移位發生時若仍用嵌入標句詞 gu(n) 會造成不合語法(如 *Co an duine a bha thu ag radh gun ...)。這顯示在中介 Spec-CP 停留不只是「可選」,而是「必須」。因此它排除了單一長跳躍的衍生方式——若允許長跳躍,就無法解釋為何用 gu 的句子不合語法。West Ulster 量詞漂浮只證明有中介位置可用,並未排除長跳躍。
answerTextEn: Because using the embedded complementizer gu(n) when wh-movement has occurred is ungrammatical (e.g. *Co an duine a bha thu ag radh gun ...). This shows stopping in the intermediate Spec-CP is not merely optional but obligatory, thereby ruling out the one-long-swoop derivation: if a long swoop were allowed, there would be no way to rule out the gu-examples. West Ulster stranding only shows an intermediate position is available; it does not rule out the long swoop.
explanationZh: 量詞漂浮是經驗論證,顯示存在停留點但不排除長跳躍;標句詞一致則因 gu 句不合語法,證明停留是強制的,從而排除長跳躍衍生。
explanationEn: Quantifier stranding is an empirical argument showing a stop-off point exists but does not exclude a long swoop; complementizer agreement, because gu-sentences are ungrammatical, proves the stop-off is obligatory, thus ruling out long-swoop derivations.

questionZh: 在西班牙語中,主語倒裝(subject inversion)與 wh-移位的關係為何?
questionEn: In Spanish, what is the relationship between subject inversion and wh-movement?

options.zh: 當 wh-詞在 Spec-CP 時,倒裝是強制的;否則為可選
options.en: When a wh-word is in Spec-CP, inversion is obligatory; otherwise it is optional

options.zh: 倒裝在所有句子中都是禁止的
options.en: Inversion is prohibited in all sentences

options.zh: 倒裝只在陳述句中發生
options.en: Inversion only occurs in declaratives

options.zh: 倒裝與 wh-移位完全無關
options.en: Inversion is entirely unrelated to wh-movement

explanationZh: 西班牙語一般主語倒裝為可選,但在 wh-問句中為強制(*Qué esos dos querían? 不合法)。概括:當 wh-詞在 Spec-CP 時倒裝強制,否則可選。
explanationEn: Spanish subject inversion is normally optional, but it is obligatory in wh-questions (*Qué esos dos querían? is ungrammatical). Generalization: when a wh-word is in Spec-CP, inversion is obligatory; otherwise optional.

questionZh: 在西班牙語長距離 wh-問句中,當最深層的賓語被 wh-移出時,主語倒裝必須在______子句中都發生,否則不合語法。
questionEn: In Spanish long-distance wh-questions, when the most embedded object is wh-extracted, subject inversion must occur in ______ clause, or the sentence is ungrammatical.
answerTextZh: 每一個(中介)
answerTextEn: each (intermediate)
explanationZh: 在例 (37) 中,主句、中介子句與最深層子句的主語都倒裝於其動詞之後;若任一子句缺少倒裝(如例 38)則不合法,證明 wh-語經過每一個中介 Spec-CP。
explanationEn: In (37) the subjects of the matrix, intermediate, and most embedded clauses all follow their verbs; if inversion is missing in any clause (as in 38) the sentence is ungrammatical, proving the wh-phrase passes through each intermediate Spec-CP.

questionZh: 依本章對特徵核對局域性的假設,在 c-統御之下,特徵核對最遠能觸及到哪裡?
questionEn: According to the chapter's hypothesis on locality in feature matching, under c-command, how far can feature checking reach?

options.zh: 不超過任何嵌入 CP(後概括為相位)的指示語
options.en: No further than the specifier of any embedded CP (later generalized to phase)
